title: How AI is helping improve heart health in rural Australia
summary: Google has launched a new AI initiative aimed at improving heart health outcomes for people living in remote Australian communities. This effort addresses healthcare access challenges in rural areas through AI-assisted diagnostic and monitoring capabilities.
content: A new Google AI initiative aims to improve heart health outcomes for people living in remote Australian communities.

zh.title: 人工智能如何帮助改善澳大利亚农村地区的心脏健康
zh.summary: 谷歌推出了一项新的AI倡议，旨在改善生活在澳大利亚偏远社区的人们的心脏健康成果。这一努力通过AI辅助诊断和监测能力解决了农村地区的医疗保健可及性挑战。

fr.title: Comment l'IA aide à améliorer la santé cardiaque en Australie rurale
fr.summary: Google a lancé une nouvelle initiative d'IA visant à améliorer les résultats de santé cardiaque pour les personnes vivant dans les communautés australiennes éloignées. Cet effort aborde les défis d'accès aux soins de santé dans les zones rurales grâce aux capacités de diagnostic et de surveillance assistées par l'IA.

de.title: Wie KI die Herzgesundheit in ländlichen Australien verbessert
de.summary: Google hat eine neue KI-Initiative gestartet, um die Herzgesundheitsergebnisse für Menschen in abgelegenen australischen Gemeinden zu verbessern. Diese Bemühung adressiert Herausforderungen beim Zugang zu Gesundheitswesen in ländlichen Gebieten durch KI-gestützte Diagnose- und Überwachungsfunktionen.

es.title: Cómo la IA está mejorando la salud cardiovascular en la Australia rural
es.summary: Google ha lanzado una nueva iniciativa de IA dirigida a mejorar los resultados de salud cardiovascular para las personas que viven en comunidades australianas remotas. Este esfuerzo aborda los desafíos de acceso a la atención médica en áreas rurales a través de capacidades de diagnóstico y monitoreo asistidas por IA.

title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump
summary: OpenClaw, a new Chinese AI tool that autonomously completes device tasks, is attracting early adopters seeking to profit from the trend, while US battery technology faces headwinds. This reflects growing interest in autonomous AI agents across emerging markets.
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze  In January, Beijing-based software engineer Feng Qingyang started tinkering with OpenClaw, a new AI tool that can take over a device and autonomously complete tasks.…

zh.title: 下载：早期采用者在中国OpenClaw热潮中获利，美国电池业陷入困境
zh.summary: OpenClaw是一款新的中国AI工具，能够自主完成设备任务，正吸引寻求从这一趋势中获利的早期采用者，而美国电池技术面临逆风。这反映了新兴市场对自主AI代理的兴趣不断增长。

fr.title: Le téléchargement : Les premiers utilisateurs font fortune dans la frénésie de OpenClaw en Chine, et les batteries américaines s'effondrent
fr.summary: OpenClaw, un nouvel outil d'IA chinois qui complète automatiquement les tâches des appareils, attire les premiers utilisateurs cherchant à profiter de la tendance, tandis que la technologie des batteries américaines fait face à des vents contraires. Cela reflète un intérêt croissant pour les agents d'IA autonomes sur les marchés émergents.

de.title: Der Download: Frühanwender verdienen mit Chinas OpenClaw-Hype, während US-Batterien zusammenbrechen
de.summary: OpenClaw ist ein neues chinesisches KI-Tool, das Geräteaufgaben autonom ausführt und zieht frühe Anwender an, die von dem Trend profitieren möchten, während die US-Batterietechnologie Gegenwind erfährt. Dies spiegelt das wachsende Interesse an autonomen KI-Agenten auf Schwellenmärkten wider.

es.title: La descarga: Los primeros en adoptar ganan dinero con la manía de OpenClaw en China, y las baterías estadounidenses se desploman
es.summary: OpenClaw, una nueva herramienta de IA china que completa autónomamente las tareas del dispositivo, está atrayendo a los primeros en adoptar que buscan beneficiarse de la tendencia, mientras que la tecnología de baterías estadounidenses enfrenta dificultades. Esto refleja un creciente interés en agentes de IA autónomos en mercados emergentes.

title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
summary: Product engineers increasingly leverage AI to enhance design and validation of everyday items from vehicles to medical devices. This demonstrates AI's expanding integration into physical products beyond traditional software applications.
content: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people alive. More and more, product engineers are turning to AI to enhance, validate, and streamline the design of the items that furnish our…

zh.title: 务实设计：为现实世界设计AI工程
zh.summary: 产品工程师越来越多地利用AI来增强从车辆到医疗设备等日常物品的设计和验证。这表明AI正在超越传统软件应用，向物理产品的扩展。

fr.title: Pragmatique par conception : Ingénierie de l'IA pour le monde réel
fr.summary: Les ingénieurs produits exploitent de plus en plus l'IA pour améliorer la conception et la validation des articles du quotidien, des véhicules aux dispositifs médicaux. Cela démontre l'intégration croissante de l'IA dans les produits physiques au-delà des applications logicielles traditionnelles.

de.title: Pragmatisch durch Design: KI-Technik für die reale Welt
de.summary: Produktingenieure nutzen zunehmend KI, um Entwurf und Validierung alltäglicher Gegenstände von Fahrzeugen bis zu Medizinprodukten zu verbessern. Dies zeigt die zunehmende Integration von KI in physischen Produkten jenseits traditioneller Softwareanwendungen.

es.title: Pragmático por diseño: Ingeniería de IA para el mundo real
es.summary: Los ingenieros de productos aprovechan cada vez más la IA para mejorar el diseño y la validación de artículos cotidianos, desde vehículos hasta dispositivos médicos. Esto demuestra la integración creciente de la IA en productos físicos más allá de las aplicaciones de software tradicionales.

title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
summary: The US battery industry is experiencing a downturn after years of explosive growth with numerous startups securing massive funding. Market conditions have shifted significantly by 2026, creating a challenging environment for companies with novel battery chemistries.
content: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds. My biggest problem was sifting through the pile to pick the most exciting news to cover. That tide has turned, and in 2026, what seems…

zh.title: 美国电池行业的困难时期
zh.summary: 美国电池行业在多年爆炸性增长和众多初创公司获得巨额融资后,正在经历低迷。到2026年,市场条件已经发生了重大变化,为拥有新型电池化学技术的公司创造了充满挑战的环境。

fr.title: Des temps difficiles pour l'industrie américaine des batteries
fr.summary: L'industrie américaine des batteries connaît une période de ralentissement après des années de croissance explosive avec de nombreuses startups obtenant des financements massifs. Les conditions du marché ont changé de manière significative d'ici 2026, créant un environnement difficile pour les entreprises possédant des chimies de batterie novatrices.

de.title: Schwierige Zeiten für die US-Batterieindustrie
de.summary: Die US-Batterieindustrie erlebt einen Abschwung nach Jahren explosiven Wachstums, bei dem zahlreiche Startups massive Finanzierungen erhielten. Die Marktbedingungen haben sich bis 2026 erheblich verschoben und schaffen eine herausfordernde Umgebung für Unternehmen mit neuartigen Batteriechemien.

es.title: Tiempos difíciles para la industria de baterías estadounidense
es.summary: La industria de baterías estadounidense está experimentando una desaceleración después de años de crecimiento explosivo con numerosas startups asegurando financiación masiva. Las condiciones del mercado han cambiado significativamente para 2026, creando un entorno desafiante para empresas con químicas de baterías novedosas.

title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
summary: Researchers propose an automated system using specialized AI agents to continuously optimize data products by surfacing relevant questions, monitoring quality metrics, and supporting human oversight. This approach reduces dependence on domain experts while making data assets more observable and refinable.
content:
arXiv:2603.10133v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Data products enable end users to gain greater insights about their data by providing supporting assets, such as example question-SQL pairs which can be answered using the data or views over the database tables. However, producing useful data products is challenging, and typically requires domain experts to hand-craft supporting assets. We propose a system that automates data product improvement through specialized AI agents operating in a continuous optimization loop. By surfacing questions, monitoring multi-dimensional quality metrics, and supporting human-in-the-loop controls, it transforms data into observable and refinable assets that balance automation with trust and oversight.

zh.title: 代理控制中心用于数据产品优化
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个自动化系统，使用专门的AI代理持续优化数据产品，通过提出相关问题、监控质量指标并支持人工监督。这种方法减少了对领域专家的依赖，同时使数据资产更加可观察和可改进。

fr.title: Centre de contrôle d'agents pour l'optimisation des produits de données
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un système automatisé utilisant des agents IA spécialisés pour optimiser continuellement les produits de données en soulevant des questions pertinentes, en surveillant les métriques de qualité et en soutenant la supervision humaine. Cette approche réduit la dépendance aux experts du domaine tout en rendant les actifs de données plus observables et améliorables.

de.title: Agentisches Kontrollzentrum zur Optimierung von Datenprodukten
de.summary: Forscher schlagen ein automatisiertes System vor, das spezialisierte KI-Agenten nutzt, um Datenprodukte kontinuierlich zu optimieren, indem es relevante Fragen aufwirft, Qualitätsmetriken überwacht und menschliche Aufsicht unterstützt. Dieser Ansatz reduziert die Abhängigkeit von Domänenexperten und macht Datenbestände beobachtbarer und verbesserbar.

es.title: Centro de control de agentes para optimización de productos de datos
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un sistema automatizado que utiliza agentes de IA especializados para optimizar continuamente los productos de datos mediante la presentación de preguntas relevantes, el monitoreo de métricas de calidad y el apoyo de supervisión humana. Este enfoque reduce la dependencia de expertos del dominio mientras hace que los activos de datos sean más observables y mejorables.

title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
summary: A new graph-based memory system called HyMEM enables GUI agents to better handle complex, long-horizon tasks by combining symbolic knowledge nodes with trajectory embeddings. The approach allows 7B-8B parameter models to match or exceed closed-source models' performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.10291v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent intermediate errors. Prior work equips agents with external memory built from large collections of trajectories, but relies on flat retrieval over discrete summaries or continuous embeddings, falling short of the structured organization and self-evolving characteristics of human memory. Inspired by the brain, we propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory that couples discrete high-level symbolic nodes with continuous trajectory embeddings. HyMEM maintains a graph structure to support multi-hop retrieval, self-evolution via node update operations, and on-the-fly working-memory refreshing during inference. Extensive experiments show that HyMEM consistently improves open-source GUI agents, enabling 7B/8B backbones to match or surpass strong closed-source models; notably, it boosts Qwen2.5-VL-7B by +22.5% and outperforms Gemini2.5-Pro-Vision and GPT-4o.

zh.title: 用于GUI代理的混合自演化结构化记忆
zh.summary: 一个名为HyMEM的新图形基础记忆系统使GUI代理能够更好地处理复杂的长期任务，通过结合符号知识节点和轨迹嵌入。这种方法允许7B-8B参数模型匹配或超越闭源模型的性能。

fr.title: Mémoire structurée auto-évolutive hybride pour les agents GUI
fr.summary: Un nouveau système de mémoire basé sur les graphes appelé HyMEM permet aux agents GUI de mieux gérer les tâches complexes et de long horizon en combinant des nœuds de connaissances symboliques avec des embeddings de trajectoire. L'approche permet aux modèles de paramètres 7B-8B de correspondre ou de dépasser les performances des modèles fermés.

de.title: Hybride selbstevoluierende strukturierte Speicher für GUI-Agenten
de.summary: Ein neues graphisches Speichersystem namens HyMEM ermöglicht es GUI-Agenten, komplexe, langfristige Aufgaben besser zu bewältigen, indem symbolische Wissensknoten mit Trajektorieneinbettungen kombiniert werden. Der Ansatz ermöglicht es Modellen mit 7B-8B-Parametern, die Leistung von Closed-Source-Modellen zu erreichen oder zu übertreffen.

es.title: Memoria estructurada auto-evolutiva híbrida para agentes GUI
es.summary: Un nuevo sistema de memoria basado en grafos llamado HyMEM permite que los agentes GUI manejen mejor tareas complejas y de largo horizonte al combinar nodos de conocimiento simbólico con incrustaciones de trayectoria. El enfoque permite que modelos de parámetros 7B-8B igualen o superen el rendimiento de modelos cerrados.

title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
summary: HEAL framework addresses the "Teacher Ceiling" problem in reasoning distillation by using entropy-assisted repairs and progressive curriculum learning to help smaller models learn from larger reasoning models. The approach significantly outperforms traditional supervised fine-tuning methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.10359v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Distilling reasoning capabilities from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into smaller models is typically constrained by the limitation of rejection sampling. Standard methods treat the teacher as a static filter, discarding complex "corner-case" problems where the teacher fails to explore valid solutions independently, thereby creating an artificial "Teacher Ceiling" for the student. In this work, we propose Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning (HEAL), an RL-free framework designed to bridge this reasoning gap. Drawing on the educational theory of the Zone of Proximal Development(ZPD), HEAL synergizes three core modules: (1) Guided Entropy-Assisted Repair (GEAR), an active intervention mechanism that detects critical reasoning breakpoints via entropy dynamics and injects targeted hindsight hints to repair broken trajectories; (2) Perplexity-Uncertainty Ratio Estimator (PURE), a rigorous filtering protocol that decouples genuine cognitive breakthroughs from spurious shortcuts; and (3) Progressive Answer-guided Curriculum Evolution (PACE), a three-stage distillation strategy that organizes training from foundational alignment to frontier breakthrough. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that HEAL significantly outperforms traditional SFT distillation and other baselines.

zh.title: HEAL：用于推理蒸馏的后见之明熵辅助学习
zh.summary: HEAL框架通过使用熵辅助修复和渐进式课程学习来解决推理蒸馏中的"教师天花板"问题，帮助较小的模型从较大的推理模型中学习。这种方法明显优于传统的监督微调方法。

fr.title: HEAL : Apprentissage assisté par l'entropie rétrospectif pour la distillation du raisonnement
fr.summary: Le cadre HEAL aborde le problème du "plafond de l'enseignant" dans la distillation du raisonnement en utilisant des réparations assistées par l'entropie et un apprentissage curriculaire progressif pour aider les modèles plus petits à apprendre des modèles de raisonnement plus grands. L'approche surpasse considérablement les méthodes traditionnelles de fine-tuning supervisé.

de.title: HEAL: Rückblick-Entropie-unterstütztes Lernen für Reasoning-Destillation
de.summary: Das HEAL-Framework adressiert das "Teacher Ceiling"-Problem bei der Reasoning-Destillation durch entropie-unterstützte Reparaturen und progressives Curriculum-Lernen, um kleineren Modellen zu helfen, von größeren Reasoning-Modellen zu lernen. Der Ansatz übertrifft traditionelle überwachte Fine-Tuning-Methoden erheblich.

es.title: HEAL: Aprendizaje asistido por entropía retrospectiva para la destilación de razonamiento
es.summary: El marco HEAL aborda el problema del "techo del docente" en la destilación de razonamiento mediante reparaciones asistidas por entropía y aprendizaje curricular progresivo para ayudar a modelos más pequeños a aprender de modelos de razonamiento más grandes. El enfoque supera significativamente los métodos tradicionales de ajuste fino supervisado.

title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
summary: The TRACED framework evaluates LLM reasoning through geometric properties—progress and stability—rather than scalar probabilities, revealing that correct reasoning follows high-progress stable trajectories while hallucinations show stalled displacement with high variability. This approach offers improved robustness in assessing model reliability.
content:
arXiv:2603.10384v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Evaluating LLM reliability via scalar probabilities often fails to capture the structural dynamics of reasoning. We introduce TRACED, a framework that assesses reasoning quality through theoretically grounded geometric kinematics. By decomposing reasoning traces into Progress (displacement) and Stability (curvature), we reveal a distinct topological divergence: correct reasoning manifests as high-progress, stable trajectories, whereas hallucinations are characterized by low-progress, unstable patterns (stalled displacement with high curvature fluctuations). Leveraging these signatures, our probabilistic framework achieves competitive performance and superior robustness across diverse benchmarks. Crucially, TRACED bridges geometry and cognition by mapping high curvature to ''Hesitation Loops'' and displacement to ''Certainty Accumulation'', offering a physical lens to decode the internal dynamics of machine thought.

zh.title: 超越标量：通过几何进度和稳定性评估和理解大语言模型推理
zh.summary: TRACED框架通过几何性质（进度和稳定性）而非标量概率来评估大语言模型推理，揭示正确推理遵循高进度稳定轨迹，而幻觉则表现为停滞位移和高变异性。这种方法在评估模型可靠性时提供了更高的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Au-delà des scalaires : évaluer et comprendre le raisonnement des LLM via la progression géométrique et la stabilité
fr.summary: Le cadre TRACED évalue le raisonnement des LLM par des propriétés géométriques—progression et stabilité—plutôt que par des probabilités scalaires, révélant que le raisonnement correct suit des trajectoires stables à haut progrès tandis que les hallucinations montrent un déplacement bloqué avec une variabilité élevée. Cette approche offre une robustesse améliorée dans l'évaluation de la fiabilité du modèle.

de.title: Jenseits von Skalaren: Bewertung und Verständnis des LLM-Denkens durch geometrischen Fortschritt und Stabilität
de.summary: Das TRACED-Framework bewertet das LLM-Denken anhand geometrischer Eigenschaften—Fortschritt und Stabilität—statt Skalarwahrscheinlichkeiten und offenbart, dass korrektes Denken stabilen Trajektorien mit hohem Fortschritt folgt, während Halluzinationen stagnierten Versatz mit hoher Variabilität zeigen. Dieser Ansatz bietet verbesserte Robustheit bei der Bewertung der Modellzuverlässigkeit.

es.title: Más allá de los escalares: Evaluación y comprensión del razonamiento de LLM a través del progreso geométrico y la estabilidad
es.summary: El marco TRACED evalúa el razonamiento de LLM a través de propiedades geométricas—progreso y estabilidad—en lugar de probabilidades escalares, revelando que el razonamiento correcto sigue trayectorias estables de alto progreso mientras que las alucinaciones muestran desplazamiento estancado con alta variabilidad. Este enfoque ofrece una robustez mejorada para evaluar la confiabilidad del modelo.

title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
summary: New uncertainty elicitation techniques based on imprecise probabilities enable LLMs to express both first-order uncertainty (over responses) and second-order uncertainty (about the probability model itself). This approach improves credibility and decision-making accuracy in ambiguous scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.10396v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Despite the growing demand for eliciting uncertainty from large language models (LLMs), empirical evidence suggests that LLM behavior is not always adequately captured by the elicitation techniques developed under the classical probabilistic uncertainty framework. This mismatch leads to systematic failure modes, particularly in settings that involve ambiguous question-answering, in-context learning, and self-reflection. To address this, we propose novel prompt-based uncertainty elicitation techniques grounded in \emph{imprecise probabilities}, a principled framework for repesenting and eliciting higher-order uncertainty. Here, first-order uncertainty captures uncertainty over possible responses to a prompt, while second-order uncertainty (uncertainty about uncertainty) quantifies indeterminacy in the underlying probability model itself. We introduce general-purpose prompting and post-processing procedures to directly elicit and quantify both orders of uncertainty, and demonstrate their effectiveness across diverse settings. Our approach enables more faithful uncertainty reporting from LLMs, improving credibility and supporting downstream decision-making.

zh.title: 通过不精确概率表达大语言模型的高阶不确定性
zh.summary: 基于不精确概率的新型不确定性提取技术使大语言模型能够表达一阶不确定性（关于响应）和二阶不确定性（关于概率模型本身）。这种方法在模糊场景中改进了可信度和决策准确性。

fr.title: Verbalisation de l'incertitude d'ordre supérieur des LLM via des probabilités imprécises
fr.summary: Les nouvelles techniques d'élicitation d'incertitude basées sur les probabilités imprécises permettent aux LLM d'exprimer à la fois l'incertitude du premier ordre (sur les réponses) et l'incertitude du second ordre (sur le modèle probabiliste lui-même). Cette approche améliore la crédibilité et la précision de la prise de décision dans les scénarios ambigus.

de.title: Verbalisierung der Unsicherheit höherer Ordnung von LLMs durch ungenaue Wahrscheinlichkeiten
de.summary: Neue Techniken zur Unsicherheitselizitation, die auf ungenauen Wahrscheinlichkeiten basieren, ermöglichen es LLMs, sowohl Unsicherheit erster Ordnung (über Antworten) als auch Unsicherheit zweiter Ordnung (über das Wahrscheinlichkeitsmodell selbst) auszudrücken. Dieser Ansatz verbessert die Glaubwürdigkeit und Genauigkeit der Entscheidungsfindung in mehrdeutigen Szenarien.

es.title: Verbalización de la incertidumbre de orden superior del LLM a través de probabilidades imprecisas
es.summary: Las nuevas técnicas de elicitación de incertidumbre basadas en probabilidades imprecisas permiten a los LLM expresar tanto incertidumbre de primer orden (sobre respuestas) como incertidumbre de segundo orden (sobre el modelo probabilístico en sí). Este enfoque mejora la credibilidad y la precisión de la toma de decisiones en escenarios ambiguos.

title: Resource-constrained Amazons chess decision framework integrating large language models and graph attention
summary: A hybrid framework combining graph attention networks with large language models enables high-performance AI for the Game of Amazons under strict resource constraints. The approach achieves 15-66% improvement over baselines and outperforms its teacher model with minimal computational resources.
content:
arXiv:2603.10512v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Artificial intelligence has advanced significantly through the development of intelligent game-playing systems, providing rigorous testbeds for decision-making, strategic planning, and adaptive learning. However, resource-constrained environments pose critical challenges, as conventional deep learning methods heavily rely on extensive datasets and computational resources. In this paper, we propose a lightweight hybrid framework for the Game of the Amazons, which explores the paradigm of weak-to-strong generalization by integrating the structural reasoning of graph-based learning with the generative capabilities of large language models. Specifically, we leverage a Graph Attention Autoencoder to inform a multi-step Monte Carlo Tree Search, utilize a Stochastic Graph Genetic Algorithm to optimize evaluation signals, and harness GPT-4o-mini to generate synthetic training data. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on expert demonstrations, our framework learns from noisy and imperfect supervision. We demonstrate that the Graph Attention mechanism effectively functions as a structural filter, denoising the LLM's outputs. Experiments on a 10$\times$10 Amazons board show that our hybrid approach not only achieves a 15\%--56\% improvement in decision accuracy over baselines but also significantly outperforms its teacher model (GPT-4o-mini), achieving a competitive win rate of 45.0\% at N=30 nodes and a decisive 66.5\% at only N=50 nodes. These results verify the feasibility of evolving specialized, high-performance game AI from general-purpose foundation models under stringent computational constraints.

zh.title: 资源受限的亚马逊象棋决策框架整合大语言模型和图注意力
zh.summary: 将图注意力网络与大语言模型相结合的混合框架在严格的资源约束下为亚马逊游戏实现了高性能人工智能。该方法相比基线获得15-66%的性能改进，并以最少的计算资源超越了其教师模型。

fr.title: Cadre de décision pour le jeu d'Amazones à ressources limitées intégrant les grands modèles de langage et l'attention graphique
fr.summary: Un cadre hybride combinant des réseaux d'attention graphique avec de grands modèles de langage permet une IA haute performance pour le jeu d'Amazones sous des contraintes de ressources strictes. L'approche réalise une amélioration de 15-66% par rapport aux modèles de base et surpasse son modèle enseignant avec des ressources informatiques minimales.

de.title: Ressourcenbeschränktes Amazons-Entscheidungsrahmen, das große Sprachmodelle und Graphenaufmerksamkeit integriert
de.summary: Ein hybrider Rahmen, der Graphen-Aufmerksamkeitsnetzwerke mit großen Sprachmodellen kombiniert, ermöglicht hochleistungsfähige KI für das Spiel der Amazonen unter strengen Ressourcenbeschränkungen. Der Ansatz erreicht eine Verbesserung von 15-66% gegenüber Basismodellen und übertrifft sein Lehrermodell mit minimalen Computerressourcen.

es.title: Marco de decisión de Amazonas con recursos limitados que integra grandes modelos de lenguaje y atención gráfica
es.summary: Un marco híbrido que combina redes de atención gráfica con grandes modelos de lenguaje permite IA de alto rendimiento para el Juego de Amazonas bajo restricciones de recursos estrictos. El enfoque logra una mejora del 15-66% sobre los modelos base y supera su modelo maestro con recursos computacionales mínimos.

title: IH-Challenge: A Training Dataset to Improve Instruction Hierarchy on Frontier LLMs
summary: OpenAI releases IH-Challenge, a training dataset that improves how frontier LLMs prioritize conflicting instructions from system prompts, developers, and users. Fine-tuning GPT-5-Mini on this dataset increases instruction hierarchy robustness by 10% and reduces unsafe behavior from 6.6% to 0.7%.
content:
arXiv:2603.10521v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Instruction hierarchy (IH) defines how LLMs prioritize system, developer, user, and tool instructions under conflict, providing a concrete, trust-ordered policy for resolving instruction conflicts. IH is key to defending against jailbreaks, system prompt extractions, and agentic prompt injections. However, robust IH behavior is difficult to train: IH failures can be confounded with instruction-following failures, conflicts can be nuanced, and models can learn shortcuts such as overrefusing. We introduce IH-Challenge, a reinforcement learning training dataset, to address these difficulties. Fine-tuning GPT-5-Mini on IH-Challenge with online adversarial example generation improves IH robustness by +10.0% on average across 16 in-distribution, out-of-distribution, and human red-teaming benchmarks (84.1% to 94.1%), reduces unsafe behavior from 6.6% to 0.7% while improving helpfulness on general safety evaluations, and saturates an internal static agentic prompt injection evaluation, with minimal capability regression. We release the IH-Challenge dataset (https://huggingface.co/datasets/openai/ih-challenge) to support future research on robust instruction hierarchy.

zh.title: IH-Challenge：改进前沿LLM指令层级的训练数据集
zh.summary: OpenAI发布IH-Challenge，一个改进前沿LLM如何优先处理来自系统提示、开发者和用户的冲突指令的训练数据集。在该数据集上微调GPT-5-Mini将指令层级鲁棒性提高10%，并将不安全行为从6.6%降低到0.7%。

fr.title: IH-Challenge : Un ensemble de données d'entraînement pour améliorer la hiérarchie des instructions sur les LLM de frontier
fr.summary: OpenAI publie IH-Challenge, un ensemble de données d'entraînement qui améliore la manière dont les LLM de frontier hiérarchisent les instructions conflictuelles en provenance des invites système, des développeurs et des utilisateurs. L'ajustement fin de GPT-5-Mini sur cet ensemble de données augmente la robustesse de la hiérarchie des instructions de 10% et réduit le comportement non sécurisé de 6,6% à 0,7%.

de.title: IH-Challenge: Ein Trainingsdatensatz zur Verbesserung der Anweisungshierarchie bei Frontier-LLMs
de.summary: OpenAI veröffentlicht IH-Challenge, einen Trainingsdatensatz, der verbessert, wie Frontier-LLMs widersprüchliche Anweisungen von Systemaufforderungen, Entwicklern und Benutzern priorisieren. Das Fine-Tuning von GPT-5-Mini auf diesem Datensatz erhöht die Robustheit der Anweisungshierarchie um 10% und reduziert unsicheres Verhalten von 6,6% auf 0,7%.

es.title: IH-Challenge: Un conjunto de datos de entrenamiento para mejorar la jerarquía de instrucciones en LLMs fronterizos
es.summary: OpenAI lanza IH-Challenge, un conjunto de datos de entrenamiento que mejora cómo los LLMs fronterizos priorizan las instrucciones conflictivas de indicaciones del sistema, desarrolladores y usuarios. El ajuste fino de GPT-5-Mini en este conjunto de datos aumenta la robustez de la jerarquía de instrucciones en un 10% y reduce el comportamiento inseguro del 6,6% al 0,7%.

title: Adaptive RAN Slicing Control via Reward-Free Self-Finetuning Agents
summary: A self-finetuning framework enables network control agents to learn continuously from interaction with their environment without handcrafted reward signals. The approach outperforms traditional reinforcement learning in handling multi-objective trade-offs in dynamic radio access networks.
content:
arXiv:2603.10564v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The integration of Generative AI models into AI-native network systems offers a transformative path toward achieving autonomous and adaptive control. However, the application of such models to continuous control tasks is impeded by intrinsic architectural limitations, including finite context windows, the lack of explicit reward signals, and the degradation of the long context. This paper posits that the key to unlocking robust continuous control is enabling agents to internalize experience by distilling it into their parameters, rather than relying on prompt-based memory. To this end, we propose a novel self-finetuning framework that enables agentic systems to learn continuously through direct interaction with the environment, bypassing the need for handcrafted rewards. Our framework implements a bi-perspective reflection mechanism that generates autonomous linguistic feedback to construct preference datasets from interaction history. A subsequent preference-based fine-tuning process distills long-horizon experiences into the model's parameters. We evaluate our approach on a dynamic Radio Access Network (RAN) slicing task, a challenging multi-objective control problem that requires the resolution of acute trade-offs between spectrum efficiency, service quality, and reconfiguration stability under volatile network conditions. Experimental results show that our framework outperforms standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) baselines and existing Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents in sample efficiency, stability, and multi-metric optimization. These findings demonstrate the potential of self-improving generative agents for continuous control tasks, paving the way for future AI-native network infrastructure.

zh.title: 通过无奖励自微调代理的自适应RAN切片控制
zh.summary: 一个自微调框架使网络控制代理能够在没有手工设计奖励信号的情况下从与环境的交互中持续学习。该方法在处理动态无线接入网络中的多目标权衡方面优于传统强化学习。

fr.title: Contrôle adaptatif du tranchage RAN via des agents d'auto-ajustement sans récompense
fr.summary: Un cadre d'auto-ajustement permet aux agents de contrôle réseau d'apprendre continuellement à partir de l'interaction avec leur environnement sans signaux de récompense conçus manuellement. L'approche surpasse l'apprentissage par renforcement traditionnel dans la gestion des compromis multi-objectifs dans les réseaux d'accès radio dynamiques.

de.title: Adaptive RAN-Schnittstellenkontrolle durch belohnungsfreie Selbst-Feinabstimmungs-Agenten
de.summary: Ein Self-Finetuning-Framework ermöglicht es Netzwerk-Kontrollakteuren, kontinuierlich aus Interaktionen mit ihrer Umgebung zu lernen, ohne handgefertigte Belohnungssignale zu verwenden. Der Ansatz übertrifft traditionelles Reinforcement Learning bei der Bewältigung von Multi-Objective-Trade-offs in dynamischen Funkzugriffsnetzwerken.

es.title: Control adaptativo de segmentación RAN mediante agentes de auto-ajuste sin recompensa
es.summary: Un marco de auto-ajuste permite que los agentes de control de red aprendan continuamente a partir de la interacción con su entorno sin señales de recompensa diseñadas manualmente. El enfoque supera al aprendizaje por refuerzo tradicional en la gestión de compensaciones multiobjeto en redes de acceso por radio dinámicas.

title: CUAAudit: Meta-Evaluation of Vision-Language Models as Auditors of Autonomous Computer-Use Agents
summary: Large-scale meta-evaluation of vision-language models as auditors for autonomous computer-use agents reveals that while state-of-the-art VLMs perform well on simple tasks, they struggle with complex or heterogeneous environments and show significant disagreement on judgments. The findings highlight fundamental limitations in model-based evaluation approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.10577v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) are emerging as a new paradigm in human-computer interaction, enabling autonomous execution of tasks in desktop environment by perceiving high-level natural-language instructions. As such agents become increasingly capable and are deployed across diverse desktop environments, evaluating their behavior in a scalable and reliable manner becomes a critical challenge. Existing evaluation pipelines rely on static benchmarks, rule-based success checks, or manual inspection, which are brittle, costly, and poorly aligned with real-world usage. In this work, we study Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as autonomous auditors for assessing CUA task completion directly from observable interactions and conduct a large-scale meta-evaluation of five VLMs that judge task success given a natural-language instruction and the final environment state. Our evaluation spans three widely used CUA benchmarks across macOS, Windows, and Linux environments and analyzes auditor behavior along three complementary dimensions: accuracy, calibration of confidence estimates, and inter-model agreement. We find that while state-of-the-art VLMs achieve strong accuracy and calibration, all auditors exhibit notable performance degradation in more complex or heterogeneous environments, and even high-performing models show significant disagreement in their judgments. These results expose fundamental limitations of current model-based auditing approaches and highlight the need to explicitly account for evaluator reliability, uncertainty, and variance when deploying autonomous CUAs in real-world settings.

zh.title: CUAAudit：视觉语言模型作为自主计算机使用代理审计员的元评估
zh.summary: 对视觉语言模型作为自主计算机使用代理审计员的大规模元评估表明，虽然最先进的VLM在简单任务上表现良好，但它们在复杂或异构环境中表现不佳，并在判断上显示出显著的分歧。这些发现突出了基于模型的评估方法的根本局限性。

fr.title: CUAAudit : Méta-évaluation des modèles de vision-langage en tant qu'auditeurs des agents autonomes d'utilisation informatique
fr.summary: Une méta-évaluation à grande échelle des modèles de vision-langage en tant qu'auditeurs pour les agents autonomes d'utilisation informatique révèle que bien que les VLM les plus avancés se comportent bien sur des tâches simples, ils ont du mal avec les environnements complexes ou hétérogènes et montrent un désaccord significatif sur les jugements. Les résultats mettent en évidence les limitations fondamentales des approches d'évaluation basées sur les modèles.

de.title: CUAAudit: Metabewertung von Vision-Sprachmodellen als Prüfer autonomer Computer-Nutzungsagenten
de.summary: Eine großangelegte Metabewertung von Vision-Sprachmodellen als Prüfer für autonome Computer-Nutzungsagenten zeigt, dass die neuesten VLMs zwar bei einfachen Aufgaben gut abschneiden, bei komplexen oder heterogenen Umgebungen jedoch Schwierigkeiten haben und bei Urteilen erhebliche Meinungsverschiedenheiten aufweisen. Die Ergebnisse unterstreichen grundlegende Einschränkungen modellgestützter Bewertungsansätze.

es.title: CUAAudit: Metaevaluación de modelos de visión-lenguaje como auditores de agentes autónomos de uso de computadora
es.summary: Una metaevaluación a gran escala de modelos de visión-lenguaje como auditores de agentes autónomos de uso de computadora revela que si bien los VLM de última generación funcionan bien en tareas simples, tienen dificultades con entornos complejos o heterogéneos y muestran un desacuerdo significativo en los juicios. Los hallazgos destacan las limitaciones fundamentales de los enfoques de evaluación basados en modelos.

title: Does LLM Alignment Really Need Diversity? An Empirical Study of Adapting RLVR Methods for Moral Reasoning
summary: Empirical comparison of distribution-matching versus reward-maximizing methods for moral reasoning finds that moral tasks contain concentrated high-reward distributions unlike mathematical reasoning. Contrary to initial hypothesis, standard reward-maximizing reinforcement learning methods prove equally or more effective for alignment tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.10588v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable success in logical reasoning tasks, yet whether large language model (LLM) alignment requires fundamentally different approaches remains unclear. Given the apparent tolerance for multiple valid responses in moral reasoning, a natural hypothesis is that alignment tasks inherently require diversity-seeking distribution-matching algorithms rather than reward-maximizing policy-based methods. We conduct the first comprehensive empirical study comparing both paradigms on MoReBench. To enable stable RLVR training, we build a rubric-grounded reward pipeline by training a Qwen3-1.7B judge model. Contrary to our hypothesis, we find that distribution-matching approaches do not demonstrate significant advantages over reward-maximizing methods as expected on alignment tasks. Through semantic visualization mapping high-reward responses to semantic space, we demonstrate that moral reasoning exhibits more concentrated high-reward distributions than mathematical reasoning, where diverse solution strategies yield similarly high rewards. This counter-intuitive finding explains why mode-seeking optimization proves equally or more effective for alignment tasks. Our results suggest that alignment tasks do not inherently require diversity-preserving algorithms, and standard reward-maximizing RLVR methods can effectively transfer to moral reasoning without explicit diversity mechanisms.

zh.title: 大语言模型对齐真的需要多样性吗？适应RLVR方法用于道德推理的实证研究
zh.summary: 分布匹配与奖励最大化方法在道德推理中的实证比较发现，道德任务包含集中的高奖励分布，不同于数学推理。与初始假设相反，标准的奖励最大化强化学习方法对对齐任务同样或更有效。

fr.title: L'alignement des LLM a-t-il vraiment besoin de diversité? Étude empirique des méthodes RLVR adaptées pour le raisonnement moral
fr.summary: Une comparaison empirique des méthodes de correspondance de distribution par rapport aux méthodes de maximisation des récompenses pour le raisonnement moral révèle que les tâches morales contiennent des distributions de récompenses élevées concentrées, contrairement au raisonnement mathématique. Contrairement à l'hypothèse initiale, les méthodes standard d'apprentissage par renforcement de maximisation des récompenses s'avèrent tout aussi ou plus efficaces pour les tâches d'alignement.

de.title: Benötigt die Ausrichtung großer Sprachmodelle wirklich Vielfalt? Eine empirische Studie zur Anpassung von RLVR-Methoden für moralisches Denken
de.summary: Ein empirischer Vergleich von Verteilungsabstimmungs- gegenüber Belohnungsmaximierungsmethoden für moralisches Denken zeigt, dass moralische Aufgaben konzentrierte hochwertige Belohnungsverteilungen enthalten, im Gegensatz zu mathematischem Denken. Entgegen der ursprünglichen Hypothese erweisen sich standardmäßige belohnungsmaximierende Reinforcement-Learning-Methoden als gleich oder effektiver für Ausrichtungsaufgaben.

es.title: ¿La alineación de LLM realmente necesita diversidad? Un estudio empírico de adaptación de métodos RLVR para razonamiento moral
es.summary: Una comparación empírica de métodos de coincidencia de distribución versus métodos de maximización de recompensas para razonamiento moral encuentra que las tareas morales contienen distribuciones de recompensas altas concentradas, a diferencia del razonamiento matemático. Contrariamente a la hipótesis inicial, los métodos estándar de aprendizaje por refuerzo de maximización de recompensas resultan ser igual o más efectivos para las tareas de alineación.

title: Trajectory-Informed Memory Generation for Self-Improving Agent Systems
summary: A framework automatically extracts actionable learnings from agent execution trajectories and injects them into future tasks through contextual memory retrieval. Evaluation shows up to 14.3 percentage point improvements in task completion, with particularly strong benefits on complex tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.10600v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-powered agents face a persistent challenge: learning from their execution experiences to improve future performance. While agents can successfully complete many tasks, they often repeat inefficient patterns, fail to recover from similar errors, and miss opportunities to apply successful strategies from past executions. We present a novel framework for automatically extracting actionable learnings from agent execution trajectories and utilizing them to improve future performance through contextual memory retrieval. Our approach comprises four components: (1) a Trajectory Intelligence Extractor that performs semantic analysis of agent reasoning patterns, (2) a Decision Attribution Analyzer that identifies which decisions and reasoning steps led to failures, recoveries, or inefficiencies, (3) a Contextual Learning Generator that produces three types of guidance -- strategy tips from successful patterns, recovery tips from failure handling, and optimization tips from inefficient but successful executions, and (4) an Adaptive Memory Retrieval System that injects relevant learnings into agent prompts based on multi-dimensional similarity. Unlike existing memory systems that store generic conversational facts, our framework understands execution patterns, extracts structured learnings with provenance, and retrieves guidance tailored to specific task contexts. Evaluation on the AppWorld benchmark demonstrates consistent improvements, with up to 14.3 percentage point gains in scenario goal completion on held-out tasks and particularly strong benefits on complex tasks (28.5~pp scenario goal improvement, a 149\% relative increase).

zh.title: 轨迹知情的记忆生成用于自我改进的代理系统
zh.summary: 一个框架自动从代理执行轨迹中提取可操作的学习，并通过上下文记忆检索将其注入到未来的任务中。评估显示任务完成率提高了14.3个百分点，在复杂任务上特别有效。

fr.title: Génération de mémoire informée par la trajectoire pour les systèmes d'agents auto-améliorants
fr.summary: Un cadre qui extrait automatiquement les apprentissages exploitables des trajectoires d'exécution des agents et les injecte dans les tâches futures par le biais de la récupération contextuelle de la mémoire. L'évaluation montre des améliorations allant jusqu'à 14,3 points de pourcentage dans la réussite des tâches, avec des avantages particulièrement importants sur les tâches complexes.

de.title: Trajektorieninformierte Speichergenerierung für selbstverbessernde Agentensysteme
de.summary: Ein Framework extrahiert automatisch umsetzbare Erkenntnisse aus Agent-Ausführungstrajektorien und injiziert sie durch kontextuelle Speicherabruf in zukünftige Aufgaben. Die Bewertung zeigt Verbesserungen von bis zu 14,3 Prozentpunkten bei der Aufgabenerledigung, mit besonders großem Nutzen bei komplexen Aufgaben.

es.title: Generación de memoria informada por trayectoria para sistemas de agentes que se mejoran a sí mismos
es.summary: Un marco que extrae automáticamente aprendizajes procesables de las trayectorias de ejecución del agente e los inyecta en tareas futuras a través de la recuperación de memoria contextual. La evaluación muestra mejoras de hasta 14,3 puntos porcentuales en la finalización de tareas, con beneficios particularmente fuertes en tareas complejas.

title: FAME: Formal Abstract Minimal Explanation for Neural Networks
summary: FAME introduces a scalable method for explaining neural network decisions using abstract interpretation and dedicated perturbation domains. The approach produces significantly smaller explanations while running faster than existing methods on medium to large-scale networks.
content:
arXiv:2603.10661v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose FAME (Formal Abstract Minimal Explanations), a new class of abductive explanations grounded in abstract interpretation. FAME is the first method to scale to large neural networks while reducing explanation size. Our main contribution is the design of dedicated perturbation domains that eliminate the need for traversal order. FAME progressively shrinks these domains and leverages LiRPA-based bounds to discard irrelevant features, ultimately converging to a formal abstract minimal explanation. To assess explanation quality, we introduce a procedure that measures the worst-case distance between an abstract minimal explanation and a true minimal explanation. This procedure combines adversarial attacks with an optional VERIX+ refinement step. We benchmark FAME against VERIX+ and demonstrate consistent gains in both explanation size and runtime on medium- to large-scale neural networks.

zh.title: FAME：神经网络的正式抽象最小解释
zh.summary: FAME引入了一个可扩展的方法来解释神经网络决策，使用抽象解释和专门的扰动域。该方法产生明显更小的解释，同时在中等到大规模网络上比现有方法运行更快。

fr.title: FAME : Explication formelle, abstraite et minimale pour les réseaux de neurones
fr.summary: FAME introduit une méthode scalable pour expliquer les décisions des réseaux de neurones en utilisant l'interprétation abstraite et les domaines de perturbation dédiés. L'approche produit des explications considérablement plus petites tout en s'exécutant plus rapidement que les méthodes existantes sur les réseaux de moyenne à grande taille.

de.title: FAME: Formale abstrakte minimale Erklärung für neuronale Netze
de.summary: FAME führt eine skalierbare Methode zur Erklärung von Entscheidungen neuronaler Netze ein, die abstrakte Interpretation und spezielle Störungsdomänen nutzt. Der Ansatz erzeugt deutlich kleinere Erklärungen und läuft schneller als bestehende Methoden bei mittelgroßen bis großen Netzwerken.

es.title: FAME: Explicación formal abstracta mínima para redes neuronales
es.summary: FAME introduce un método escalable para explicar las decisiones de redes neuronales utilizando interpretación abstracta y dominios de perturbación dedicados. El enfoque produce explicaciones significativamente más pequeñas mientras se ejecuta más rápido que los métodos existentes en redes de mediano a gran escala.

title: Emulating Clinician Cognition via Self-Evolving Deep Clinical Research
summary: DxEvolve, a self-evolving diagnostic agent, improves clinical diagnosis accuracy by 11.2% through interactive deep clinical research that continuously externalizes expertise as reusable diagnostic primitives. The system demonstrates comparable performance to clinician reference standards on evaluation benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.10677v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Clinical diagnosis is a complex cognitive process, grounded in dynamic cue acquisition and continuous expertise accumulation. Yet most current artificial intelligence (AI) systems are misaligned with this reality, treating diagnosis as single-pass retrospective prediction while lacking auditable mechanisms for governed improvement. We developed DxEvolve, a self-evolving diagnostic agent that bridges these gaps through an interactive deep clinical research workflow. The framework autonomously requisitions examinations and continually externalizes clinical experience from increasing encounter exposure as diagnostic cognition primitives. On the MIMIC-CDM benchmark, DxEvolve improved diagnostic accuracy by 11.2% on average over backbone models and reached 90.4% on a reader-study subset, comparable to the clinician reference (88.8%). DxEvolve improved accuracy on an independent external cohort by 10.2% (categories covered by the source cohort) and 17.1% (uncovered categories) compared to the competitive method. By transforming experience into a governable learning asset, DxEvolve supports an accountable pathway for the continual evolution of clinical AI.

zh.title: 通过自我演进的深度临床研究模拟临床医生认知
zh.summary: DxEvolve是一个自我演进的诊断代理，通过持续将专业知识外化为可复用的诊断原始元素的交互式深度临床研究，将临床诊断准确率提高了11.2%。该系统在评估基准上展示了与临床医生参考标准相当的性能。

fr.title: Émuler la Cognition Clinicienne via la Recherche Clinique Profonde Auto-Évolutive
fr.summary: DxEvolve, un agent de diagnostic auto-évolutif, améliore la précision du diagnostic clinique de 11,2% grâce à une recherche clinique profonde interactive qui externalise continuellement l'expertise en primitives diagnostiques réutilisables. Le système démontre des performances comparables aux normes de référence des cliniciens sur les critères d'évaluation.

de.title: Klinische Kognition durch selbstentwickelnde tiefe klinische Forschung emulieren
de.summary: DxEvolve, ein selbstentwickelndes Diagnosesystem, verbessert die Genauigkeit der klinischen Diagnose um 11,2% durch interaktive tiefe klinische Forschung, die Fachwissen kontinuierlich als wiederverwendbare diagnostische Primitive externalisiert. Das System zeigt vergleichbare Leistungen mit klinischen Referenzstandards bei Bewertungsmaßstäben.

es.title: Emulación de la Cognición Clínica a través de Investigación Clínica Profunda Autoevolutiva
es.summary: DxEvolve, un agente de diagnóstico autoevaluable, mejora la precisión del diagnóstico clínico en un 11,2% mediante investigación clínica profunda interactiva que externaliza continuamente la experiencia como primitivos diagnósticos reutilizables. El sistema demuestra un rendimiento comparable a los estándares de referencia clínica en puntos de referencia de evaluación.

title: Nurture-First Agent Development: Building Domain-Expert AI Agents Through Conversational Knowledge Crystallization
summary: Nurture-First Development proposes building domain-expert AI agents through progressive interaction with practitioners rather than code-first or prompt-first approaches. The paradigm recognizes domain expertise as tacit and evolving, consolidating fragmented knowledge into structured, reusable assets.
content:
arXiv:2603.10808v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The emergence of large language model (LLM)-based agent frameworks has shifted the primary challenge in building domain-expert AI agents from raw capability to effective encoding of domain expertise. Two dominant paradigms -- code-first development, which embeds expertise in deterministic pipelines, and prompt-first development, which captures expertise in static system prompts -- both treat agent construction as a discrete engineering phase preceding deployment. We argue that this sequential assumption creates a fundamental mismatch with the nature of domain expertise, which is substantially tacit, deeply personal, and continuously evolving. We propose Nurture-First Development (NFD), a paradigm in which agents are initialized with minimal scaffolding and progressively grown through structured conversational interaction with domain practitioners. The central mechanism is the Knowledge Crystallization Cycle, whereby fragmented knowledge embedded in operational dialogue is periodically consolidated into structured, reusable knowledge assets. We formalize NFD through: (1) a Three-Layer Cognitive Architecture organizing agent knowledge by volatility and personalization degree; (2) the Knowledge Crystallization Cycle with formal definitions of crystallization operations and efficiency metrics; and (3) an operational framework comprising a Dual-Workspace Pattern and Spiral Development Model. We illustrate the paradigm through a detailed case study on building a financial research agent for U.S. equity analysis and discuss the conditions, limitations, and broader implications of NFD for human-agent co-evolution.

zh.title: 培育式代理开发：通过对话知识结晶化构建领域专家AI代理
zh.summary: 培育式开发提出通过与实践者的渐进交互而不是代码优先或提示优先方法来构建领域专家AI代理。该范例认识到领域专业知识是隐性的、不断演进的，将碎片化知识整合为结构化、可复用的资产。

fr.title: Développement d'Agents Nourris : Construire des Agents IA Experts en Domaine par Cristallisation Conversationnelle des Connaissances
fr.summary: Le Développement Nourri propose de construire des agents IA experts en domaine par interaction progressive avec les praticiens plutôt que par des approches code-first ou prompt-first. Le paradigme reconnaît que l'expertise en domaine est tacite et évolutive, consolidant les connaissances fragmentées en actifs structurés et réutilisables.

de.title: Pflegliche Agentwicklung: Aufbau von Domain-Expert-KI-Agenten durch konversationelle Wissenskristellisierung
de.summary: Pflegliche Entwicklung schlägt vor, Domain-Expert-KI-Agenten durch progressive Interaktion mit Praktizierenden statt durch Code-First- oder Prompt-First-Ansätze zu entwickeln. Das Paradigma erkennt Domänenexpertise als implizit und sich entwickelnd an und konsolidiert fragmentiertes Wissen in strukturierte, wiederverwendbare Vermögenswerte.

es.title: Desarrollo de Agentes Nutridos: Construyendo Agentes de IA Expertos en Dominio a Través de Cristalización Conversacional del Conocimiento
es.summary: Desarrollo Nutrido propone construir agentes IA expertos en dominio mediante interacción progresiva con profesionales en lugar de enfoques code-first o prompt-first. El paradigma reconoce la experiencia en dominio como tácita y en evolución, consolidando conocimiento fragmentado en activos estructurados y reutilizables.

title: A Hybrid Knowledge-Grounded Framework for Safety and Traceability in Prescription Verification
summary: PharmGraph-Auditor combines a hybrid pharmaceutical knowledge base with LLM-based chain-of-verification reasoning to enable safe, transparent prescription auditing. The system addresses medication error risks by grounding LLM decisions in verifiable knowledge rather than raw generation.
content:
arXiv:2603.10891v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Medication errors pose a significant threat to patient safety, making pharmacist verification (PV) a critical, yet heavily burdened, final safeguard. The direct application of Large Language Models (LLMs) to this zero-tolerance domain is untenable due to their inherent factual unreliability, lack of traceability, and weakness in complex reasoning. To address these challenges, we introduce PharmGraph-Auditor, a novel system designed for safe and evidence-grounded prescription auditing. The core of our system is a trustworthy Hybrid Pharmaceutical Knowledge Base (HPKB), implemented under the Virtual Knowledge Graph (VKG) paradigm. This architecture strategically unifies a relational component for set constraint satisfaction and a graph component for topological reasoning via a rigorous mapping layer. To construct this HPKB, we propose the Iterative Schema Refinement (ISR) algorithm, a framework that enables the co-evolution of both graph and relational schemas from medical texts. For auditing, we introduce the KB-grounded Chain of Verification (CoV), a new reasoning paradigm that transforms the LLM from an unreliable generator into a transparent reasoning engine. CoV decomposes the audit task into a sequence of verifiable queries against the HPKB, generating hybrid query plans to retrieve evidence from the most appropriate data store. Experimental results demonstrate robust knowledge extraction capabilities and show promises of using PharmGraph-Auditor to enable pharmacists to achieve safer and faster prescription verification.

zh.title: 处方验证中安全性和可追溯性的混合知识基础框架
zh.summary: PharmGraph-Auditor结合了混合制药知识库与基于LLM的链式验证推理，以实现安全、透明的处方审计。该系统通过将LLM决策基于可验证的知识而非原始生成来解决药物错误风险。

fr.title: Un Cadre Hybride Fondé sur les Connaissances pour la Sécurité et la Traçabilité dans la Vérification des Ordonnances
fr.summary: PharmGraph-Auditor combine une base de connaissances pharmaceutique hybride avec un raisonnement de chaîne de vérification basé sur les LLM pour permettre un audit transparent et sûr des ordonnances. Le système aborde les risques d'erreurs médicamenteuses en fondant les décisions des LLM sur des connaissances vérifiables plutôt que sur une génération brute.

de.title: Ein hybrids wissensgestütztes Framework für Sicherheit und Rückverfolgbarkeit in der Rezeptüberprüfung
de.summary: PharmGraph-Auditor kombiniert eine hybride pharmazeutische Wissensdatenbank mit LLM-basierter Kettenüberprüfungs-Reasoning, um sichere und transparente Rezeptprüfungen zu ermöglichen. Das System behebt Medikamentenfehlerrisiken, indem es LLM-Entscheidungen in überprüfbarem Wissen statt roher Generierung verankert.

es.title: Un Marco Híbrido Fundamentado en Conocimiento para Seguridad y Trazabilidad en Verificación de Prescripciones
es.summary: PharmGraph-Auditor combina una base de conocimiento farmacéutico híbrida con razonamiento de cadena de verificación basado en LLM para permitir auditoría de prescripciones segura y transparente. El sistema aborda los riesgos de errores de medicamentos basando las decisiones de LLM en conocimiento verificable en lugar de generación cruda.

title: Decoupling Reasoning and Confidence: Resurrecting Calibration in Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
summary: This paper addresses overconfidence issues in LLMs trained with RLVR by proposing DCPO, a framework that decouples reasoning and calibration optimization objectives. The approach preserves accuracy while achieving superior confidence calibration and robustness against adversarial scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.09117v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances large language models (LLMs) reasoning but severely suffers from calibration degeneration, where models become excessively over-confident in incorrect answers. Previous studies devote to directly incorporating calibration objective into existing optimization target. However, our theoretical analysis demonstrates that there exists a fundamental gradient conflict between the optimization for maximizing policy accuracy and minimizing calibration error. Building on this insight, we propose DCPO, a simple yet effective framework that systematically decouples reasoning and calibration objectives. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DCPO not only preserves accuracy on par with GRPO but also achieves the best calibration performance and substantially mitigates the over-confidence issue. Our study provides valuable insights and practical solution for more reliable LLM deployment.

zh.title: 解耦推理与置信度:在可验证奖励强化学习中复兴校准
zh.summary: 本文通过提出DCPO框架来解决使用RLVR训练的大语言模型中的过度置信问题,该框架将推理和校准优化目标解耦。该方法在保持准确性的同时,实现了更优的置信度校准和对对抗情景的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Découpler le Raisonnement et la Confiance: Ressusciter l'Étalonnage dans l'Apprentissage par Renforcement à partir de Récompenses Vérifiables
fr.summary: Cet article aborde les problèmes de surconfiance dans les LLMs entraînés avec RLVR en proposant DCPO, un cadre qui découple les objectifs d'optimisation du raisonnement et de l'étalonnage. L'approche préserve la précision tout en réalisant un étalonnage de confiance supérieur et une robustesse contre les scénarios adversariaux.

de.title: Entkopplung von Reasoning und Konfidenz: Wiederbelebung der Kalibrierung im Reinforcement Learning aus verifizierbaren Belohnungen
de.summary: Dieses Papier befasst sich mit Übervertrauensproblemen in mit RLVR trainierten LLMs, indem es DCPO vorschlägt, ein Framework, das Reasoning- und Kalibrierungsoptimierungsziele entkoppelt. Der Ansatz bewahrt die Genauigkeit, während er eine überlegene Konfidenzkalibrierung und Robustheit gegen gegnerische Szenarien erreicht.

es.title: Desacoplamiento de Razonamiento y Confianza: Resucitando la Calibración en Aprendizaje por Refuerzo de Recompensas Verificables
es.summary: Este artículo aborda problemas de excesiva confianza en modelos de lenguaje grandes entrenados con RLVR al proponer DCPO, un marco que desacopla objetivos de optimización de razonamiento y calibración. El enfoque preserva la precisión mientras logra una calibración de confianza superior y robustez contra escenarios adversariales.

title: One Model, Many Skills: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Multitask Code Analysis
summary: A comprehensive evaluation shows that parameter-efficient fine-tuning can effectively handle multiple code analysis tasks using a single shared module. The research demonstrates that multi-task PEFT achieves comparable accuracy to single-task fine-tuning while reducing storage and computation costs by up to 85%.
content:
arXiv:2603.09978v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models have recently surpassed specialized systems on code generation, yet their effectiveness on other code-analysis tasks remains less clear. At the same time, multi-task learning offers a way to unify diverse objectives within a single model, but fully fine-tuning LLMs across tasks is computationally prohibitive. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning mitigates this cost by updating only a small fraction of weights. Although PEFT has proven effective in single-task settings, its potential for multi-task learning has not yet been systematically explored. We present the first comprehensive evaluation of multi-task PEFT for code analysis, comparing several methods across diverse tasks and model architectures. Our experiments show that a single PEFT module shared across tasks can match, and in some cases surpass, full multi-task fine-tuning, confirming that the benefits of PEFT extend beyond isolated tasks. When comparing single-task and multi-task setups, we find that multi-task PEFT achieves a favorable performance-efficiency trade-off: it delivers accuracy close to single-task fine-tuning while reducing storage requirements, cutting the number of trainable parameters by a factor of the task count, and lowering computation costs by as much as 85%. At the same time, multi-task gains remain sensitive to task grouping. Through task-pairing experiments, we identify key factors shaping outcomes: task stability, model architecture, task complementarity, asymmetry, and dataset quality determine the success of co-fine-tuning. Finally, we benchmark efficient multi-task PEFT against direct prompting of open-source general-purpose LLMs, including DeepSeek, Qwen, Mistral, CodeLlama, and StarCoder. Despite their strong performance in code generation, these models underperform on analysis tasks, where even a 1B-parameter model with multi-task PEFT achieves significantly better results.

zh.title: 一个模型,多种技能:用于多任务代码分析的参数高效微调
zh.summary: 全面的评估表明,参数高效微调可以使用单个共享模块有效处理多个代码分析任务。研究证明多任务PEFT相比单任务微调可以达到可比的准确性,同时将存储和计算成本降低多达85%。

fr.title: Un Modèle, Plusieurs Compétences: Ajustement Fin Efficace en Paramètres pour l'Analyse de Code Multitâche
fr.summary: Une évaluation complète montre que l'ajustement fin efficace en paramètres peut gérer efficacement plusieurs tâches d'analyse de code en utilisant un module partagé unique. La recherche démontre que le PEFT multi-tâche réalise une précision comparable à l'ajustement fin single-task tout en réduisant les coûts de stockage et de calcul jusqu'à 85%.

de.title: Ein Modell, Viele Fähigkeiten: Parametereffizientes Fine-Tuning für mehrtätige Codeanalyse
de.summary: Eine umfassende Bewertung zeigt, dass parametereffizientes Fine-Tuning mehrere Codeanalyse-Aufgaben effektiv mit einem einzigen gemeinsamen Modul bewältigen kann. Die Forschung zeigt, dass Multi-Task-PEFT eine mit Single-Task-Fine-Tuning vergleichbare Genauigkeit erreicht, während Speicher- und Rechenkosten um bis zu 85% gesenkt werden.

es.title: Un Modelo, Muchas Habilidades: Ajuste Fino Eficiente en Parámetros para Análisis de Código Multitarea
es.summary: Una evaluación completa muestra que el ajuste fino eficiente en parámetros puede manejar efectivamente múltiples tareas de análisis de código usando un módulo compartido único. La investigación demuestra que PEFT multitarea logra una precisión comparable al ajuste fino de tarea única mientras reduce los costos de almacenamiento y computación hasta en un 85%.

title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
summary: The study proposes targeted reasoning unlearning (TRU), which uses reasoning-based guidance to remove undesirable knowledge from LLMs while preserving general capabilities. TRU achieves more reliable knowledge removal and demonstrates superior robustness compared to existing gradient ascent methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.09980v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: LLM unlearning is essential for mitigating safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained large language models (LLMs). Compared to preference alignment, it offers a more explicit way by removing undesirable knowledge characterized by specific unlearning datasets. In previous works, gradient ascent (GA) and its variants have shown promise for implementing unlearning, yet their untargeted nature results in unintended degradation of general capabilities, incomplete removal of knowledge, and the generation of incoherent responses, among many others. We argue that these issues stem from the absence of explicit guidance on what and how models should unlearn. To fill this gap, we introduce a novel unlearning target, reasoning-based unlearning target, which satisfies both the specified unlearning scope and the specified post-unlearning response. Building on this, we propose targeted reasoning unlearning (TRU), which leverages reasoning-based unlearning target as guidance. We employ the target using a cross-entropy supervised loss combined with a GA-based loss, enabling the model to learn reasoning ability for precise knowledge removal while preserving unrelated abilities. We evaluate TRU against strong baselines across multiple benchmarks and LLM backbones, and find that it achieves more reliable unlearning while preserving general capabilities. Moreover, TRU exhibits superior robustness under diverse attack scenarios, stemming from the reasoning ability learned through reasoning-based targets. Overall, our study establishes reasoning-augmented unlearning as a practical paradigm for reliable and explainable LLM unlearning.

zh.title: 可解释的LLM推理遗忘
zh.summary: 该研究提出了目标推理遗忘(TRU),使用基于推理的指导从LLM中移除不良知识,同时保持一般能力。TRU实现了更可靠的知识删除,与现有梯度上升方法相比表现出更强的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Oubli d'LLM explicable par le raisonnement
fr.summary: L'étude propose l'oubli de raisonnement ciblé (TRU), qui utilise des conseils basés sur le raisonnement pour supprimer les connaissances indésirables des LLM tout en préservant les capacités générales. TRU atteint une suppression de connaissances plus fiable et démontre une robustesse supérieure par rapport aux méthodes existantes de montée en gradient.

de.title: Erklärbares LLM-Vergessen durch Reasoning
de.summary: Die Studie schlägt gezieltes Reasoning-Vergessen (TRU) vor, das Reasoning-basierte Anleitung verwendet, um unerwünschtes Wissen aus LLMs zu entfernen und gleichzeitig allgemeine Fähigkeiten zu bewahren. TRU erreicht eine zuverlässigere Wissensentfernung und demonstriert überlegene Robustheit im Vergleich zu bestehenden Gradient-Ascent-Methoden.

es.title: Desaprendizaje explainable de LLM a través del razonamiento
es.summary: El estudio propone el desaprendizaje de razonamiento específico (TRU), que utiliza orientación basada en razonamiento para eliminar conocimientos indeseables de los LLM mientras preserva capacidades generales. TRU logra una eliminación de conocimientos más confiable y demuestra una robustez superior en comparación con métodos de ascenso de gradiente existentes.

title: AraModernBERT: Transtokenized Initialization and Long-Context Encoder Modeling for Arabic
summary: AraModernBERT adapts the ModernBERT architecture for Arabic with transtokenized initialization and native support for sequences up to 8,192 tokens. Transtokenization proves essential for Arabic language modeling, enabling improved performance on downstream NLU and sequence labeling tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09982v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Encoder-only transformer models remain widely used for discriminative NLP tasks, yet recent architectural advances have largely focused on English. In this work, we present AraModernBERT, an adaptation of the ModernBERT encoder architecture to Arabic, and study the impact of transtokenized embedding initialization and native long-context modeling up to 8,192 tokens. We show that transtokenization is essential for Arabic language modeling, yielding dramatic improvements in masked language modeling performance compared to non-transtokenized initialization. We further demonstrate that AraModernBERT supports stable and effective long-context modeling, achieving improved intrinsic language modeling performance at extended sequence lengths. Downstream evaluations on Arabic natural language understanding tasks, including inference, offensive language detection, question-question similarity, and named entity recognition, confirm strong transfer to discriminative and sequence labeling settings. Our results highlight practical considerations for adapting modern encoder architectures to Arabic and other languages written in Arabic-derived scripts.

zh.title: AraModernBERT:阿拉伯语长上下文编码器的转词初始化
zh.summary: AraModernBERT使用转词初始化将ModernBERT架构改编为阿拉伯语,并原生支持最多8,192个令牌的序列。转词化证明对阿拉伯语语言建模至关重要,在下游自然语言理解和序列标记任务上实现了改进的性能。

fr.title: AraModernBERT: Initialisation transtokenisée et encodeur long-contexte pour l'arabe
fr.summary: AraModernBERT adapte l'architecture ModernBERT pour l'arabe avec une initialisation transtokenisée et un support natif pour les séquences jusqu'à 8 192 tokens. La transtokenisation s'avère essentielle pour la modélisation du langage arabe, permettant une performance améliorée sur les tâches de compréhension du langage naturel et d'étiquetage de séquence en aval.

de.title: AraModernBERT: Transtoken-Initialisierung und Long-Context-Encoder-Modellierung für Arabisch
de.summary: AraModernBERT passt die ModernBERT-Architektur für Arabisch mit transtokenisierter Initialisierung und nativer Unterstützung für Sequenzen mit bis zu 8.192 Tokens an. Transtokenisierung erweist sich als wesentlich für die arabische Sprachmodellierung und ermöglicht verbesserte Leistung bei nachgelagerten NLU- und Sequenz-Labeling-Aufgaben.

es.title: AraModernBERT: Inicialización transtokenizada y codificador de largo contexto para árabe
es.summary: AraModernBERT adapta la arquitectura ModernBERT para árabe con inicialización transtokenizada y soporte nativo para secuencias de hasta 8.192 tokens. La transtokenización resulta esencial para el modelado del lenguaje árabe, permitiendo un rendimiento mejorado en tareas de comprensión del lenguaje natural y etiquetado de secuencias posteriores.

title: MoE-SpAc: Efficient MoE Inference Based on Speculative Activation Utility in Heterogeneous Edge Scenarios
summary: MoE-SpAc introduces an inference framework for Mixture-of-Experts models on edge devices using speculative decoding to predict expert memory demand. The system achieves 42% throughput improvement over existing approaches and up to 4x speedup through optimized memory management.
content:
arXiv:2603.09983v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models enable scalable performance but face severe memory constraints on edge devices. Existing offloading strategies struggle with I/O bottlenecks due to the dynamic, low-information nature of autoregressive expert activation. In this paper, we propose to repurpose Speculative Decoding (SD) not merely as a compute accelerator, but as an informative lookahead sensor for memory management, supported by our theoretical and empirical analyses. Hence, we introduce MoE-SpAc, an MoE inference framework that integrates a Speculative Utility Estimator to track expert demand, a Heterogeneous Workload Balancer to dynamically partition computation via online integer optimization, and an Asynchronous Execution Engine to unify the prefetching and eviction in the same utility space. Extensive experiments on seven benchmarks demonstrate that MoE-SpAc achieves a 42% improvement in TPS over the SOTA SD-based baseline, and an average 4.04x speedup over all standard baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/lshAlgorithm/MoE-SpAc .

zh.title: MoE-SpAc:异构边缘场景中基于推测激活效用的高效MoE推理
zh.summary: MoE-SpAc为边缘设备上的混合专家模型引入了推理框架,使用推测解码预测专家内存需求。该系统在现有方法上实现了42%的吞吐量改进,并通过优化的内存管理实现了高达4倍的加速。

fr.title: MoE-SpAc: Inférence MoE efficace basée sur l'utilité d'activation spéculative dans des scénarios périphériques hétérogènes
fr.summary: MoE-SpAc introduit un cadre d'inférence pour les modèles Mixture-of-Experts sur les appareils périphériques en utilisant le décodage spéculatif pour prédire la demande mémoire des experts. Le système atteint une amélioration de 42 % du débit par rapport aux approches existantes et une accélération jusqu'à 4x grâce à la gestion mémoire optimisée.

de.title: MoE-SpAc: Effiziente MoE-Inferenz basierend auf spekulativer Aktivierungsnutzung in heterogenen Edge-Szenarien
de.summary: MoE-SpAc führt ein Inferenzrahmenwerk für Mixture-of-Experts-Modelle auf Edge-Geräten ein, das spekulative Dekodierung verwendet, um die Speicheranforderungen von Experten vorherzusagen. Das System erreicht eine 42%ige Durchsatzverbesserung gegenüber bestehenden Ansätzen und eine bis zu 4-fache Beschleunigung durch optimierte Speicherverwaltung.

es.title: MoE-SpAc: Inferencia eficiente de MoE basada en utilidad de activación especulativa en escenarios de borde heterogéneo
es.summary: MoE-SpAc introduce un marco de inferencia para modelos Mixture-of-Experts en dispositivos edge usando decodificación especulativa para predecir la demanda de memoria de expertos. El sistema logra una mejora de rendimiento del 42% sobre enfoques existentes y una aceleración de hasta 4x a través de una gestión de memoria optimizada.

title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models: An Empirical Study of Confidence Calibration
summary: An empirical study of four state-of-the-art LLMs reveals Dunning-Kruger-like patterns, where poorly performing models exhibit severe overconfidence. Claude Haiku 4.5 achieves the best calibration with ECE of 0.122 and 75.4% accuracy, while other models show significantly higher calibration errors.
content:
arXiv:2603.09985v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet their ability to accurately assess their own confidence remains poorly understood. We present an empirical study investigating whether LLMs exhibit patterns reminiscent of the Dunning-Kruger effect -- a cognitive bias where individuals with limited competence tend to overestimate their abilities. We evaluate four state-of-the-art models (Claude Haiku 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Flash, and Kimi K2) across four benchmark datasets totaling 24,000 experimental trials. Our results reveal striking calibration differences: Kimi K2 exhibits severe overconfidence with an Expected Calibration Error (ECE) of 0.726 despite only 23.3% accuracy, while Claude Haiku 4.5 achieves the best calibration (ECE = 0.122) with 75.4% accuracy. These findings demonstrate that poorly performing models display markedly higher overconfidence -- a pattern analogous to the Dunning-Kruger effect in human cognition. We discuss implications for safe deployment of LLMs in high-stakes applications.

zh.title: 大语言模型中的邓宁-克鲁格效应：置信度校准的实证研究
zh.summary: 对四个最先进大语言模型的实证研究揭示了类似邓宁-克鲁格的模式，表现较差的模型表现出严重过度自信。Claude Haiku 4.5以ECE为0.122和75.4%的准确率实现了最佳校准，而其他模型显示出显著更高的校准误差。

fr.title: L'Effet Dunning-Kruger dans les Grands Modèles de Langage: Une Étude Empirique de l'Étalonnage de la Confiance
fr.summary: Une étude empirique de quatre LLM de pointe révèle des modèles de type Dunning-Kruger, où les modèles peu performants présentent une confiance excessive. Claude Haiku 4.5 atteint le meilleur étalonnage avec une ECE de 0,122 et une précision de 75,4%, tandis que d'autres modèles montrent des erreurs d'étalonnage significativement plus élevées.

de.title: Der Dunning-Kruger-Effekt in Großen Sprachmodellen: Eine empirische Studie der Kalibrierung von Konfidenz
de.summary: Eine empirische Studie von vier modernen LLMs offenbart Dunning-Kruger-ähnliche Muster, bei denen leistungsschwache Modelle schwerwiegende Überconfidence zeigen. Claude Haiku 4.5 erreicht die beste Kalibrierung mit ECE von 0,122 und 75,4% Genauigkeit, während andere Modelle deutlich höhere Kalibrierungsfehler aufweisen.

es.title: El Efecto Dunning-Kruger en Modelos de Lenguaje Grandes: Un Estudio Empírico de Calibración de Confianza
es.summary: Un estudio empírico de cuatro LLM de última generación revela patrones similares a Dunning-Kruger, donde los modelos de bajo rendimiento muestran una confianza excesiva severa. Claude Haiku 4.5 logra la mejor calibración con ECE de 0,122 y precisión del 75,4%, mientras que otros modelos muestran errores de calibración significativamente más altos.

title: Quantifying Hallucinations in Language Language Models on Medical Textbooks
summary: Medical question-answering evaluation reveals that LLaMA-70B-Instruct hallucinated in 19.7% of answers despite high perceived plausibility when grounded in textbooks. Clinician assessments show strong correlation between lower hallucination rates and higher model usefulness scores.
content:
arXiv:2603.09986v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Hallucinations, the tendency for large language models to provide responses with factually incorrect and unsupported claims, is a serious problem within natural language processing for which we do not yet have an effective solution to mitigate against. Existing benchmarks for medical QA rarely evaluate this behavior against a fixed evidence source. We ask how often hallucinations occur on textbook-grounded QA and how responses to medical QA prompts vary across models. We conduct two experiments: the first experiment to determine the prevalence of hallucinations for a prominent open source large language model (LLaMA-70B-Instruct) in medical QA given novel prompts, and the second experiment to determine the prevalence of hallucinations and clinician preference to model responses. We observed, in experiment one, with the passages provided, LLaMA-70B-Instruct hallucinated in 19.7\% of answers (95\% CI 18.6 to 20.7) even though 98.8\% of prompt responses received maximal plausibility, and observed in experiment two, across models, lower hallucination rates aligned with higher usefulness scores ($\rho=-0.71$, $p=0.058$).
