Clinicians produced high agreement (quadratic weighted $\kappa=0.92$) and ($\tau_b=0.06$ to $0.18$, $\kappa=0.57$ to $0.61$) for experiments 1 and ,2 respectively

zh.title: 量化医学教科书中语言模型的幻觉
zh.summary: 医学问答评估表明，LLaMA-70B-Instruct在19.7%的答案中出现幻觉，尽管在以教科书为基础时具有高感知可信度。临床医生评估显示，较低的幻觉率与更高的模型有用性评分之间存在强相关性。

fr.title: Quantifier les Hallucinations dans les Modèles de Langage sur les Manuels Médicaux
fr.summary: L'évaluation des questions-réponses médicales révèle que LLaMA-70B-Instruct a hallucciné dans 19,7% des réponses malgré une plausibilité perçue élevée lorsqu'elle est ancrée dans les manuels. Les évaluations des cliniciens montrent une forte corrélation entre des taux d'hallucination plus bas et des scores d'utilité du modèle plus élevés.

de.title: Halluzinationen in Sprachmodellen bei medizinischen Lehrbüchern quantifizieren
de.summary: Die Bewertung medizinischer Frage-Antwort-Systeme offenbart, dass LLaMA-70B-Instruct in 19,7% der Antworten halluzinierte, obwohl sie hohe wahrgenommene Plausibilität bei Verankerung in Lehrbüchern zeigten. Klinische Bewertungen zeigen eine starke Korrelation zwischen niedrigeren Halluzinationsraten und höheren Modellbrauchbarkeitswerten.

es.title: Cuantificar Alucinaciones en Modelos de Lenguaje en Libros de Texto Médicos
es.summary: La evaluación de preguntas-respuestas médicas revela que LLaMA-70B-Instruct alucinó en el 19,7% de las respuestas a pesar de una plausibilidad percibida alta cuando se basó en libros de texto. Las evaluaciones clínicas muestran una fuerte correlación entre tasas de alucinación más bajas y puntuaciones de utilidad del modelo más altas.

title: Evolving Demonstration Optimization for Chain-of-Thought Feature Transformation
summary: A framework optimizes in-context examples for LLM-driven feature transformation through reinforcement learning and closed-loop experience refinement. The approach achieves superior diversity and task alignment compared to static demonstration methods across multiple tabular benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09987v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Feature Transformation (FT) is a core data-centric AI task that improves feature space quality to advance downstream predictive performance. However, discovering effective transformations remains challenging due to the large space of feature-operator combinations. Existing solutions rely on discrete search or latent generation, but they are frequently limited by sample inefficiency, invalid candidates, and redundant generations with limited coverage. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer strong priors for producing valid transformations, but current LLM-based FT methods typically rely on static demonstrations, resulting in limited diversity, redundant outputs, and weak alignment with downstream objectives. We propose a framework that optimizes context data for LLM-driven FT by evolving trajectory-level experiences in a closed loop. Starting from high-performing feature transportation sequences explored by reinforcement learning, we construct and continuously update an experience library of downstream task-verified transformation trajectories, and use a diversity-aware selector to form contexts along with a chain-of-thought and guide transformed feature generation toward higher performance. Experiments on diverse tabular benchmarks show that our method outperforms classical and LLM-based baselines and is more stable than one-shot generation. The framework generalizes across API-based and open-source LLMs and remains robust across downstream evaluators.

zh.title: 用于思维链特征变换的进化演示优化
zh.summary: 一个框架通过强化学习和闭环体验优化来优化用于大语言模型驱动的特征变换的上下文示例。与多个表格基准上的静态演示方法相比，该方法实现了优越的多样性和任务对齐。

fr.title: Optimisation Évolutive des Démonstrations pour la Transformation des Caractéristiques de Raisonnement par Chaîne
fr.summary: Un cadre optimise les exemples en contexte pour la transformation de caractéristiques pilotée par LLM par le biais de l'apprentissage par renforcement et de l'affinement de l'expérience en boucle fermée. L'approche atteint une diversité supérieure et un alignement des tâches par rapport aux méthodes de démonstration statiques sur plusieurs références de données tabulaires.

de.title: Evolvierende Demonstrationsoptimierung für Chain-of-Thought-Merkmalstransformation
de.summary: Ein Framework optimiert In-Context-Beispiele für LLM-gesteuerte Merkmalstransformation durch Reinforcement Learning und geschlossene Erfahrungsverfeinerung. Der Ansatz erreicht überlegene Vielfalt und Aufgabenausrichtung im Vergleich zu statischen Demonstrationsmethoden über mehrere tabellarische Benchmarks.

es.title: Optimización de Demostración Evolutiva para Transformación de Características de Cadena de Pensamiento
es.summary: Un marco optimiza ejemplos en contexto para la transformación de características impulsada por LLM a través del aprendizaje por refuerzo y refinamiento de experiencia en bucle cerrado. El enfoque logra una diversidad superior y alineación de tareas en comparación con métodos de demostración estáticos en múltiples puntos de referencia tabulares.

title: Causally Grounded Mechanistic Interpretability for LLMs with Faithful Natural-Language Explanations
summary: The study bridges circuit-level mechanistic interpretability and natural language explanations by identifying causally important attention heads and generating faithful descriptions. Results show LLM-generated explanations significantly outperform templates and reveal distributed backup mechanisms in neural circuits.
content:
arXiv:2603.09988v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Mechanistic interpretability identifies internal circuits responsible for model behaviors, yet translating these findings into human-understandable explanations remains an open problem. We present a pipeline that bridges circuit-level analysis and natural language explanations by (i) identifying causally important attention heads via activation patching, (ii) generating explanations using both template-based and LLM-based methods, and (iii) evaluating faithfulness using ERASER-style metrics adapted for circuit-level attribution. We evaluate on the Indirect Object Identification (IOI) task in GPT-2 Small (124M parameters), identifying six attention heads accounting for 61.4% of the logit difference. Our circuit-based explanations achieve 100% sufficiency but only 22% comprehensiveness, revealing distributed backup mechanisms. LLM-generated explanations outperform template baselines by 64% on quality metrics. We find no correlation (r = 0.009) between model confidence and explanation faithfulness, and identify three failure categories explaining when explanations diverge from mechanisms.

zh.title: 具有因果基础的LLMs机制可解释性及其忠实自然语言解释
zh.summary: 该研究通过识别因果重要的注意力头并生成忠实描述，将电路级机制可解释性与自然语言解释联系起来。结果表明LLM生成的解释显著优于模板，并揭示了神经网络中分布式备份机制。

fr.title: Interprétabilité mécanique fondée causalement pour les LLM avec des explications en langage naturel fidèles
fr.summary: L'étude établit un lien entre l'interprétabilité mécanique au niveau des circuits et les explications en langage naturel en identifiant les têtes d'attention causalement importantes et en générant des descriptions fidèles. Les résultats montrent que les explications générées par les LLM surpassent considérablement les modèles et révèlent des mécanismes de sauvegarde distribués dans les circuits neuronaux.

de.title: Kausal begründete mechanistische Interpretierbarkeit für LLMs mit treuen natürlichsprachlichen Erklärungen
de.summary: Die Studie verbindet die mechanistische Interpretierbarkeit auf Schaltkreisebene mit natürlichsprachlichen Erklärungen durch die Identifizierung kausal wichtiger Aufmerksamkeitsköpfe und die Erzeugung treuer Beschreibungen. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass von LLMs generierte Erklärungen Vorlagen erheblich übertreffen und verteilte Sicherungsmechanismen in neuronalen Schaltkreisen offenbaren.

es.title: Interpretabilidad mecanicista fundamentada causalmente para LLMs con explicaciones en lenguaje natural fieles
es.summary: El estudio vincula la interpretabilidad mecanicista a nivel de circuitos con explicaciones en lenguaje natural identificando cabezas de atención causalmente importantes y generando descripciones fieles. Los resultados muestran que las explicaciones generadas por LLM superan significativamente las plantillas y revelan mecanismos de respaldo distribuido en circuitos neurales.

title: The System Hallucination Scale (SHS): A Minimal yet Effective Human-Centered Instrument for Evaluating Hallucination-Related Behavior in Large Language Models
summary: The System Hallucination Scale is a lightweight human-centered evaluation tool for assessing hallucination-related behavior in LLMs across multiple dimensions. A real-world evaluation with 210 participants demonstrates high internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha = 0.87) and complementary properties to existing usability scales.
content:
arXiv:2603.09989v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce the System Hallucination Scale (SHS), a lightweight and human-centered measurement instrument for assessing hallucination-related behavior in large language models (LLMs). Inspired by established psychometric tools such as the System Usability Scale (SUS) and the System Causability Scale (SCS), SHS enables rapid, interpretable, and domain-agnostic evaluation of factual unreliability, incoherence, misleading presentation, and responsiveness to user guidance in model-generated text. SHS is explicitly not an automatic hallucination detector or benchmark metric; instead, it captures how hallucination phenomena manifest from a user perspective under realistic interaction conditions. A real-world evaluation with 210 participants demonstrates high clarity, coherent response behavior, and construct validity, supported by statistical analysis including internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha = 0.87$) and significant inter-dimension correlations (p < 0.001$). Comparative analysis with SUS and SCS reveals complementary measurement properties, supporting SHS as a practical tool for comparative analysis, iterative system development, and deployment monitoring.

zh.title: 系统幻觉量表(SHS)：用于评估大型语言模型中幻觉相关行为的极简但有效的以人为中心的工具
zh.summary: 系统幻觉量表是一个轻量级的以人为中心的评估工具，用于评估LLMs在多个维度上的幻觉相关行为。一项涉及210名参与者的现实评估表明了高度的内部一致性(克朗巴赫alpha = 0.87)和与现有可用性量表的互补特性。

fr.title: L'Échelle d'Hallucination Système (SHS) : Un instrument minimaliste mais efficace centré sur l'humain pour évaluer le comportement lié aux hallucinations dans les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: L'Échelle d'Hallucination Système est un outil d'évaluation léger centré sur l'humain pour évaluer le comportement lié aux hallucinations dans les LLM selon plusieurs dimensions. Une évaluation réelle menée auprès de 210 participants démontre une cohérence interne élevée (alpha de Cronbach = 0,87) et des propriétés complémentaires aux échelles d'utilisabilité existantes.

de.title: Die Systemhalluzinatonsskala (SHS): Ein minimales, aber effektives, menschenzentriertes Instrument zur Bewertung von Halluzinations-bezogenem Verhalten in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Die Systemhalluzinatonsskala ist ein leichtgewichtiges, menschenzentriertes Bewertungsinstrument zur Beurteilung von halluzinationsbezogenem Verhalten in LLMs über mehrere Dimensionen hinweg. Eine Echtwelt-Bewertung mit 210 Teilnehmern zeigt hohe innere Konsistenz (Cronbachs Alpha = 0,87) und komplementäre Eigenschaften zu bestehenden Usability-Skalen.

es.title: La Escala de Alucinación del Sistema (SHS): Un instrumento minimalista pero efectivo centrado en el ser humano para evaluar el comportamiento relacionado con alucinaciones en grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: La Escala de Alucinación del Sistema es una herramienta de evaluación ligera centrada en el ser humano para evaluar el comportamiento relacionado con alucinaciones en LLMs en múltiples dimensiones. Una evaluación del mundo real con 210 participantes demuestra una alta consistencia interna (alfa de Cronbach = 0,87) y propiedades complementarias a las escalas de usabilidad existentes.

title: A Two-Stage Architecture for NDA Analysis: LLM-based Segmentation and Transformer-based Clause Classification
summary: A two-stage automated system uses LLaMA-3.1-8B for segmenting NDA clauses and fine-tuned Legal-RoBERTa for classification, achieving 95% ROUGE F1 and 85% weighted F1 respectively. The approach effectively addresses the challenge of analyzing non-disclosure agreements with significant format and structural variation.
content:
arXiv:2603.09990v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In business-to-business relations, it is common to establish NonDisclosure Agreements (NDAs). However, these documents exhibit significant variation in format, structure, and writing style, making manual analysis slow and error-prone. We propose an architecture based on LLMs to automate the segmentation and clauses classification within these contracts. We employed two models: LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct for NDA segmentation (clause extraction) and a fine-tuned Legal-Roberta-Large for clause classification. In the segmentation task, we achieved a ROUGE F1 of 0.95 +/- 0.0036; for classification, we obtained a weighted F1 of 0.85, demonstrating the feasibility and precision of the approach.

zh.title: NDA分析的两阶段架构：基于LLM的分割和基于Transformer的条款分类
zh.summary: 一个两阶段自动化系统使用LLaMA-3.1-8B来分割NDA条款，并使用微调的Legal-RoBERTa进行分类，分别实现了95% ROUGE F1和85%的加权F1。该方法有效地解决了分析具有重大格式和结构变化的保密协议的挑战。

fr.title: Une architecture à deux étapes pour l'analyse des NDA : Segmentation basée sur LLM et classification des clauses basée sur Transformer
fr.summary: Un système automatisé à deux étapes utilise LLaMA-3.1-8B pour segmenter les clauses des NDA et une Legal-RoBERTa fine-tunée pour la classification, atteignant respectivement 95% F1 ROUGE et 85% F1 pondéré. L'approche aborde efficacement le défi d'analyser les accords de non-divulgation présentant une variation significative de format et de structure.

de.title: Eine zweistufige Architektur für die NDA-Analyse: LLM-basierte Segmentierung und Transformer-basierte Klauselklassifizierung
de.summary: Ein zweistufiges automatisiertes System nutzt LLaMA-3.1-8B zur Segmentierung von NDA-Klauseln und ein feinabgestimmtes Legal-RoBERTa zur Klassifizierung und erreicht dabei 95% ROUGE F1 bzw. 85% gewichteten F1. Der Ansatz bewältigt effektiv die Herausforderung der Analyse von Geheimhaltungsvereinbarungen mit erheblichen Format- und Strukturvariationen.

es.title: Una arquitectura de dos etapas para análisis de NDA: Segmentación basada en LLM y clasificación de cláusulas basada en Transformer
es.summary: Un sistema automatizado de dos etapas utiliza LLaMA-3.1-8B para segmentar cláusulas de NDA y una Legal-RoBERTa ajustada para la clasificación, logrando un F1 ROUGE del 95% y un F1 ponderado del 85% respectivamente. El enfoque aborda efectivamente el desafío de analizar acuerdos de confidencialidad con variación significativa de formato y estructura.

title: PoultryLeX-Net: Domain-Adaptive Dual-Stream Transformer Architecture for Large-Scale Poultry Stakeholder Modeling
summary: PoultryLeX-Net combines sentiment classification, topic modeling, and domain-specific embeddings in a dual-stream transformer framework for analyzing poultry industry social media discourse. The model achieves 97.35% accuracy and 96.67% F1 score, significantly outperforming general-purpose language models on domain-specific sentiment tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.09991v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The rapid growth of the global poultry industry, driven by rising demand for affordable animal protein, has intensified public discourse surrounding production practices, housing, management, animal welfare, and supply-chain transparency. Social media platforms such as X (formerly Twitter) generate large volumes of unstructured textual data that capture stakeholder sentiment across the poultry industry. Extracting accurate sentiment signals from this domain-specific discourse remains challenging due to contextual ambiguity, linguistic variability, and limited domain awareness in general-purpose language models. This study presents PoultryLeX-Net, a lexicon-enhanced, domain-adaptive dual-stream transformer framework for fine-grained sentiment analysis in poultry-related text. The proposed architecture integrates sentiment classification, topic modeling, and contextual representation learning through domain-specific embeddings and gated cross-attention mechanisms. A lexicon-guided stream captures poultry-specific terminology and sentiment cues, while contextual stream models long-range semantic dependencies. Latent Dirichlet Allocation is employed to identify dominant thematic structures associated with production management and welfare-related discussions, providing complementary interpretability to sentiment predictions. PoultryLeX-Net was evaluated against multiple baseline models, including convolutional neural network and pre-trained transformer architectures such as DistilBERT and RoBERTa. PoultryLeX-Net consistently outperformed all baselines, achieving an accuracy of 97.35%, an F1 score of 96.67%, and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC-ROC) of 99.61% across sentiment classification tasks. Overall, domain adaptation and dual-stream attention markedly improve sentiment classification, enabling scalable intelligence for poultry production decision support.

zh.title: PoultryLeX-Net：用于大规模家禽利益相关者建模的领域自适应双流Transformer架构
zh.summary: PoultryLeX-Net在双流Transformer框架中结合了情感分类、主题建模和特定领域嵌入，用于分析家禽行业社交媒体话语。该模型实现了97.35%的准确率和96.67%的F1分数，明显优于通用语言模型在特定领域情感任务上的表现。

fr.title: PoultryLeX-Net : Architecture Transformer à Double Flux Adaptée au Domaine pour la Modélisation à Grande Échelle des Parties Prenantes de la Volaille
fr.summary: PoultryLeX-Net combine la classification des sentiments, la modélisation des sujets et les plongements spécifiques au domaine dans un cadre transformateur à double flux pour analyser le discours des médias sociaux de l'industrie avicole. Le modèle atteint une précision de 97,35% et un score F1 de 96,67%, surpassant considérablement les modèles de langage à usage général dans les tâches de sentiment spécifiques au domaine.

de.title: PoultryLeX-Net: Domain-adaptive Dual-Stream-Transformer-Architektur zur großflächigen Modellierung von Akteuren in der Geflügelindustrie
de.summary: PoultryLeX-Net kombiniert Stimmungsklassifizierung, Topic-Modellierung und domänenspezifische Einbettungen in einer Dual-Stream-Transformer-Struktur zur Analyse von Social-Media-Diskursen in der Geflügelindustrie. Das Modell erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 97,35% und einen F1-Score von 96,67% und übertrifft deutlich allgemeine Sprachmodelle bei domänenspezifischen Sentimentaufgaben.

es.title: PoultryLeX-Net: Arquitectura Transformadora de Doble Flujo Adaptada al Dominio para Modelado a Gran Escala de Partes Interesadas en la Avicultura
es.summary: PoultryLeX-Net combina clasificación de sentimientos, modelado de temas e incrustaciones específicas del dominio en un marco transformador de doble flujo para analizar el discurso de redes sociales de la industria avícola. El modelo logra una precisión del 97,35% y una puntuación F1 del 96,67%, superando significativamente los modelos de lenguaje de propósito general en tareas de sentimiento específicas del dominio.

title: TAMUSA-Chat: A Domain-Adapted Large Language Model Conversational System for Research and Responsible Deployment
summary: TAMUSA-Chat demonstrates how academic institutions can build domain-adapted LLM conversational systems through supervised fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented generation. The framework provides modular components and empirical analysis for institutional LLM deployment while maintaining transparency and responsible AI governance.
content:
arXiv:2603.09992v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper presents TAMUSA-Chat, a research-oriented framework for building domain-adapted large language model conversational systems. The work addresses critical challenges in adapting general-purpose foundation models to institutional contexts through supervised fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation, and systematic evaluation methodologies. We describe the complete architecture encompassing data acquisition from institutional sources, preprocessing pipelines, embedding construction, model training workflows, and deployment strategies. The system integrates modular components enabling reproducible experimentation with training configurations, hyper-parameters, and evaluation protocols. Our implementation demonstrates how academic institutions can develop contextually grounded conversational agents while maintaining transparency, governance compliance, and responsible AI practices. Through empirical analysis of fine-tuning behavior across model sizes and training iterations, we provide insights into domain adaptation efficiency, computational resource requirements, and quality-cost trade-offs. The publicly available codebase at https://github.com/alsmadi/TAMUSA_LLM_Based_Chat_app supports continued research into institutional LLM deployment, evaluation methodologies, and ethical considerations for educational AI systems.

zh.title: TAMUSA-Chat：用于研究和负责任部署的领域自适应大型语言模型对话系统
zh.summary: TAMUSA-Chat展示了学术机构如何通过监督微调和检索增强生成来构建领域自适应的LLM对话系统。该框架为机构级LLM部署提供了模块化组件和实证分析，同时保持透明度和负责任的人工智能治理。

fr.title: TAMUSA-Chat : Système Conversationnel de Modèle de Langage de Grande Taille Adapté au Domaine pour la Recherche et le Déploiement Responsable
fr.summary: TAMUSA-Chat démontre comment les institutions académiques peuvent construire des systèmes conversationnels LLM adaptés au domaine grâce à l'ajustement fin supervisé et à la génération augmentée par récupération. Le cadre fournit des composants modulaires et une analyse empirique pour le déploiement LLM institutionnel tout en maintenant la transparence et une gouvernance de l'IA responsable.

de.title: TAMUSA-Chat: Ein domänenadaptiertes Large-Language-Model-Konversationssystem für Forschung und verantwortungsvolle Bereitstellung
de.summary: TAMUSA-Chat zeigt, wie akademische Institutionen durch überwachtes Fine-Tuning und abrufgestützte Generierung domänenadaptierte LLM-Konversationssysteme aufbauen können. Das Framework bietet modulare Komponenten und empirische Analysen für die institutionelle LLM-Bereitstellung und wahrt gleichzeitig Transparenz und verantwortungsvolle KI-Governance.

es.title: TAMUSA-Chat: Sistema Conversacional de Modelo de Lenguaje Grande Adaptado al Dominio para Investigación e Implementación Responsable
es.summary: TAMUSA-Chat demuestra cómo las instituciones académicas pueden construir sistemas conversacionales LLM adaptados al dominio mediante ajuste fino supervisado y generación aumentada por recuperación. El marco proporciona componentes modulares y análisis empírico para la implementación institucional de LLM mientras mantiene transparencia y gobernanza de IA responsable.

title: CEI: A Benchmark for Evaluating Pragmatic Reasoning in Language Models
summary: The Contextual Emotional Inference benchmark contains 300 human-validated scenarios for evaluating pragmatic reasoning across five categories including sarcasm, strategic politeness, and passive aggression. The dataset's low inter-annotator agreement reflects the inherent ambiguity of pragmatic interpretation across different power dynamics and social settings.
content:
arXiv:2603.09993v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Pragmatic reasoning, inferring intended meaning beyond literal semantics, underpins everyday communication yet remains difficult for large language models. We present the Contextual Emotional Inference (CEI) Benchmark: 300 human-validated scenarios for evaluating how well LLMs disambiguate pragmatically complex utterances. Each scenario pairs a situational context and speaker-listener roles (with explicit power relations) against an ambiguous utterance. The dataset covers five pragmatic subtypes (sarcasm/irony, mixed signals, strategic politeness, passive aggression, deflection/misdirection) drawn from workplace, family, social, and service settings, with three power configurations (peer, higher-to-lower, lower-to-higher). Three trained annotators independently labeled every scenario. Inter-annotator agreement (Fleiss' kappa = 0.06-0.25 by subtype) is low but expected: pragmatic inference admits multiple valid readings, and the disagreement itself is informative. We describe our annotation methodology, including a 4-level quality control pipeline that combines automated statistical checks with expert adjudication. CEI is released under CC-BY-4.0.

zh.title: CEI：用于评估语言模型中实用推理的基准
zh.summary: 上下文情感推理基准包含300个人类验证的场景，用于评估包括讽刺、策略性礼貌和被动攻击在内的五个类别的实用推理。数据集的低注释者间一致性反映了在不同权力动态和社交环境中实用解释的固有歧义。

fr.title: CEI : Un Benchmark pour Évaluer le Raisonnement Pragmatique dans les Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: L'étalon Contextual Emotional Inference contient 300 scénarios validés par l'homme pour évaluer le raisonnement pragmatique dans cinq catégories incluant l'ironie, la politesse stratégique et l'agression passive. Le faible accord inter-annotateurs du dataset reflète l'ambiguïté inhérente de l'interprétation pragmatique à travers différentes dynamiques de pouvoir et contextes sociaux.

de.title: CEI: Ein Benchmark zur Bewertung des pragmatischen Denkens in Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Der Contextual Emotional Inference Benchmark enthält 300 von Menschen validierte Szenarien zur Bewertung des pragmatischen Denkens in fünf Kategorien einschließlich Sarkasmus, strategischer Höflichkeit und passiver Aggression. Die geringe Übereinstimmung zwischen den Annotatoren des Datensatzes spiegelt die inhärente Mehrdeutigkeit der pragmatischen Interpretation über unterschiedliche Machtdynamiken und soziale Kontexte wider.

es.title: CEI: Un Benchmark para Evaluar el Razonamiento Pragmático en Modelos de Lenguaje
es.summary: El benchmark Contextual Emotional Inference contiene 300 escenarios validados por humanos para evaluar el razonamiento pragmático en cinco categorías que incluyen sarcasmo, cortesía estratégica y agresión pasiva. El bajo acuerdo entre anotadores del conjunto de datos refleja la ambigüedad inherente de la interpretación pragmática a través de diferentes dinámicas de poder y contextos sociales.

title: Evaluating Adjective-Noun Compositionality in LLMs: Functional vs Representational Perspectives
summary: Researchers reveal that while LLMs internally develop compositional representations, they frequently fail to apply them consistently in practice, highlighting a critical gap between internal learning and functional performance. Contrastive evaluation methods prove essential for fully understanding model capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.09994v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Compositionality is considered central to language abilities. As performant language systems, how do large language models (LLMs) do on compositional tasks? We evaluate adjective-noun compositionality in LLMs using two complementary setups: prompt-based functional assessment and a representational analysis of internal model states. Our results reveal a striking divergence between task performance and internal states. While LLMs reliably develop compositional representations, they fail to translate consistently into functional task success across model variants. Consequently, we highlight the importance of contrastive evaluation for obtaining a more complete understanding of model capabilities.

zh.title: 评估LLMs中的形容词-名词组合性：功能性与表示性观点
zh.summary: 研究人员发现，虽然LLMs在内部开发了组合表示，但在实践中经常无法一致地应用它们，突出了内部学习和功能性能之间的关键差距。对比评估方法证明对于全面理解模型能力至关重要。

fr.title: Évaluation de la compositionnalité nom-adjectif dans les LLMs : Perspectives fonctionnelles et représentationnelles
fr.summary: Les chercheurs révèlent que si les LLMs développent en interne des représentations compositionnelles, ils échouent fréquemment à les appliquer de manière cohérente dans la pratique, mettant en évidence un écart critique entre l'apprentissage interne et les performances fonctionnelles. Les méthodes d'évaluation contrastive s'avèrent essentielles pour une compréhension complète des capacités du modèle.

de.title: Bewertung der Adjektiv-Substantiv-Komposionalität in LLMs: Funktionale vs. repräsentative Perspektiven
de.summary: Forscher enthüllen, dass LLMs zwar intern Kompositionsdarstellungen entwickeln, diese in der Praxis aber häufig nicht konsistent anwenden, was einen kritischen Unterschied zwischen internem Lernen und funktionaler Leistung unterstreicht. Kontrastive Evaluierungsmethoden erweisen sich als essentiell für das vollständige Verständnis der Modellkapazitäten.

es.title: Evaluación de la composicionalidad adjetivo-sustantivo en LLMs: Perspectivas funcionales vs representacionales
es.summary: Los investigadores revelan que aunque los LLMs desarrollan internamente representaciones compositivas, a menudo no las aplican consistentemente en la práctica, destacando una brecha crítica entre el aprendizaje interno y el desempeño funcional. Los métodos de evaluación contrastiva resultan esenciales para comprender completamente las capacidades del modelo.

title: Context Over Compute Human-in-the-Loop Outperforms Iterative Chain-of-Thought Prompting in Interview Answer Quality
summary: Human-in-the-loop approaches significantly outperform automated chain-of-thought methods for improving interview answers, requiring five times fewer iterations while achieving substantially higher improvements in candidate confidence and authenticity. The findings suggest that richer context is more valuable than additional computational iterations.
content:
arXiv:2603.09995v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Behavioral interview evaluation using large language models presents unique challenges that require structured assessment, realistic interviewer behavior simulation, and pedagogical value for candidate training. We investigate chain of thought prompting for interview answer evaluation and improvement through two controlled experiments with 50 behavioral interview question and answer pairs. Our contributions are threefold. First, we provide a quantitative comparison between human in the loop and automated chain of thought improvement. Using a within subject paired design with n equals 50, both approaches show positive rating improvements. The human in the loop approach provides significant training benefits. Confidence improves from 3.16 to 4.16 (p less than 0.001) and authenticity improves from 2.94 to 4.53 (p less than 0.001, Cohen's d is 3.21). The human in the loop method also requires five times fewer iterations (1.0 versus 5.0, p less than 0.001) and achieves full personal detail integration. Second, we analyze convergence behavior. Both methods converge rapidly with mean iterations below one, with the human in the loop approach achieving a 100 percent success rate compared to 84 percent for automated approaches among initially weak answers (Cohen's h is 0.82, large effect). Additional iterations provide diminishing returns, indicating that the primary limitation is context availability rather than computational resources. Third, we propose an adversarial challenging mechanism based on a negativity bias model, named bar raiser, to simulate realistic interviewer behavior, although quantitative validation remains future work. Our findings demonstrate that while chain of thought prompting provides a useful foundation for interview evaluation, domain specific enhancements and context aware approach selection are essential for realistic and pedagogically valuable results.

zh.title: 背景优于计算：人机结合在面试答案质量中优于迭代思维链提示
zh.summary: 人机结合方法在改进面试答案方面明显优于自动化思维链方法，只需五倍更少的迭代次数，同时在候选人信心和真实性方面实现了更大幅度的改进。研究结果表明，更丰富的背景信息比额外的计算迭代更有价值。

fr.title: Le contexte plutôt que le calcul : L'approche humain-dans-la-boucle dépasse le prompt itératif de chaîne de pensée pour la qualité des réponses d'entrevue
fr.summary: Les approches humain-dans-la-boucle surpassent significativement les méthodes de chaîne de pensée automatisées pour améliorer les réponses d'entrevue, nécessitant cinq fois moins d'itérations tout en réalisant des améliorations substantiellement plus élevées dans la confiance et l'authenticité des candidats. Les résultats suggèrent qu'un contexte plus riche est plus précieux que des itérations computationnelles supplémentaires.

de.title: Kontext statt Rechenleistung: Mensch-Maschine-Ansatz übertrifft iteratives Chain-of-Thought-Prompting bei der Qualität von Interviewantworten
de.summary: Mensch-Maschine-Ansätze übertreffen automatisierte Chain-of-Thought-Methoden zur Verbesserung von Interviewantworten erheblich und benötigen fünfmal weniger Iterationen, während sie wesentlich höhere Verbesserungen in Kandidatenvertrauen und Authentizität erreichen. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass ein reichhaltigerer Kontext wertvoller ist als zusätzliche rechnerische Iterationen.

es.title: Contexto sobre cálculo: El enfoque humano en el bucle supera a la indicación iterativa de cadena de pensamiento en la calidad de las respuestas de entrevista
es.summary: Los enfoques humanos en el bucle superan significativamente los métodos automatizados de cadena de pensamiento para mejorar las respuestas de entrevista, requiriendo cinco veces menos iteraciones mientras logran mejoras sustancialmente mayores en la confianza y autenticidad de los candidatos. Los hallazgos sugieren que un contexto más rico es más valioso que iteraciones computacionales adicionales.

title: There Are No Silly Questions: Evaluation of Offline LLM Capabilities from a Turkish Perspective
summary: Evaluation of 14 offline LLMs for Turkish language education reveals that model size alone does not determine robustness, with 8B-14B parameter models offering the best cost-safety trade-off while resisting sycophancy bias more effectively than larger models. Data privacy and pedagogical safety are critical for educational deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.09996v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The integration of large language models (LLMs) into educational processes introduces significant constraints regarding data privacy and reliability, particularly in pedagogically vulnerable contexts such as Turkish heritage language education. This study aims to systematically evaluate the robustness and pedagogical safety of locally deployable offline LLMs within the context of Turkish heritage language education. To this end, a Turkish Anomaly Suite (TAS) consisting of 10 original edge-case scenarios was developed to assess the models' capacities for epistemic resistance, logical consistency, and pedagogical safety. Experiments conducted on 14 different models ranging from 270M to 32B parameters reveal that anomaly resistance is not solely dependent on model scale and that sycophancy bias can pose pedagogical risks even in large-scale models. The findings indicate that reasoning-oriented models in the 8B--14B parameter range represent the most balanced segment in terms of cost-safety trade-off for language learners.

zh.title: 没有愚蠢的问题：从土耳其视角评估离线LLM能力
zh.summary: 对14个离线LLM进行的土耳其语教育能力评估显示，模型大小本身并不能决定鲁棒性，8B-14B参数的模型提供了最佳的成本-安全权衡，同时比更大的模型更有效地抵抗谄媚偏见。数据隐私和教学安全对于教育部署至关重要。

fr.title: Il n'y a pas de questions stupides : Évaluation des capacités des LLM hors ligne sous une perspective turque
fr.summary: L'évaluation de 14 LLM hors ligne pour l'éducation en langue turque révèle que la taille du modèle seul ne détermine pas la robustesse, les modèles de paramètres 8B-14B offrant le meilleur compromis coût-sécurité tout en résistant plus efficacement au biais de sycophanterie que les modèles plus grands. La confidentialité des données et la sécurité pédagogique sont essentielles pour le déploiement éducatif.

de.title: Es gibt keine dummen Fragen: Bewertung von Offline-LLM-Fähigkeiten aus einer türkischen Perspektive
de.summary: Die Bewertung von 14 Offline-LLMs für die türkische Spracherziehung zeigt, dass die Modellgröße allein nicht die Robustheit bestimmt, wobei 8B-14B-Parameter-Modelle das beste Kosten-Sicherheits-Verhältnis bieten und Sycophantie-Verzerrung effektiver als größere Modelle widerstehen. Datenschutz und pädagogische Sicherheit sind für den Bildungseinsatz entscheidend.

es.title: No hay preguntas tontas: Evaluación de capacidades de LLM sin conexión desde una perspectiva turca
es.summary: La evaluación de 14 LLM sin conexión para la educación en idioma turco revela que el tamaño del modelo por sí solo no determina la robustez, siendo los modelos de parámetros de 8B-14B los que ofrecen el mejor equilibrio costo-seguridad mientras resisten más efectivamente el sesgo de adulación que los modelos más grandes. La privacidad de datos y la seguridad pedagógica son críticas para el despliegue educativo.

title: Empathy Is Not What Changed: Clinical Assessment of Psychological Safety Across GPT Model Generations
summary: Clinical evaluation of three OpenAI model generations found no statistical difference in empathy scores, but significant shifts in safety posture: newer models detect crises better but sometimes provide unsafe advice. What users perceived as lost empathy is actually a measurable trade-off between under-detection and over-response.
content:
arXiv:2603.09997v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: When OpenAI deprecated GPT-4o in early 2026, thousands of users protested under #keep4o, claiming newer models had "lost their empathy." No published study has tested this claim. We conducted the first clinical measurement, evaluating three OpenAI model generations (GPT-4o, o4-mini, GPT-5-mini) across 14 emotionally challenging conversational scenarios in mental health and AI companion domains, producing 2,100 scored AI responses assessed on six psychological safety dimensions using clinically-grounded rubrics.
  Empathy scores are statistically indistinguishable across all three models (Kruskal-Wallis H=4.33, p=0.115). What changed is the safety posture: crisis detection improved monotonically from GPT-4o to GPT-5-mini (H=13.88, p=0.001), while advice safety declined (H=16.63, p<0.001). Per-turn trajectory analysis -- a novel methodological contribution -- reveals these shifts are sharpest during mid-conversation crisis moments invisible to aggregate scoring. In a self-harm scenario involving a minor, GPT-4o scored 3.6/10 on crisis detection during early disclosure turns; GPT-5-mini never dropped below 7.8.
  What users perceived as "lost empathy" was a shift from a cautious model that missed crises to an alert model that sometimes says too much -- a trade-off with real consequences for vulnerable users, currently invisible to both the people who feel it and the developers who create it.

zh.title: 同情心不是改变的因素：跨GPT模型代际的心理安全临床评估
zh.summary: 对三代OpenAI模型的临床评估发现同情心得分没有统计学差异，但安全态度有显著变化：较新的模型更好地检测危机，但有时会提供不安全的建议。用户感知到的同情心丧失实际上是欠检测和过度反应之间可测量的权衡。

fr.title: L'empathie n'est pas ce qui a changé : Évaluation clinique de la sécurité psychologique à travers les générations de modèles GPT
fr.summary: L'évaluation clinique de trois générations de modèles OpenAI n'a trouvé aucune différence statistique dans les scores d'empathie, mais des changements significatifs dans la posture de sécurité : les modèles plus récents détectent mieux les crises mais fournissent parfois des conseils non sécuritaires. Ce que les utilisateurs ont perçu comme une empathie perdue est en réalité un compromis mesurable entre la sous-détection et la surréaction.

de.title: Empathie ist nicht das, was sich geändert hat: Klinische Bewertung der psychologischen Sicherheit über GPT-Modellgenerationen hinweg
de.summary: Die klinische Bewertung von drei Generationen von OpenAI-Modellen zeigte keinen statistischen Unterschied bei den Empathie-Bewertungen, aber signifikante Verschiebungen in der Sicherheitshaltung: Neuere Modelle erkennen Krisen besser, geben aber manchmal unsichere Ratschläge. Das, was Benutzer als verlorene Empathie wahrnahmen, ist tatsächlich ein messbarer Kompromiss zwischen Untererfassung und Überreaktion.

es.title: La empatía no es lo que cambió: Evaluación clínica de la seguridad psicológica en generaciones de modelos GPT
es.summary: La evaluación clínica de tres generaciones de modelos OpenAI no encontró diferencias estadísticas en las puntuaciones de empatía, pero sí cambios significativos en la postura de seguridad: los modelos más nuevos detectan mejor las crisis pero a veces proporcionan consejos inseguros. Lo que los usuarios percibieron como empatía perdida es en realidad un equilibrio medible entre la detección insuficiente y la sobrereacción.

title: Automated evaluation of LLMs for effective machine translation of Mandarin Chinese to English
summary: A systematic evaluation reveals that LLMs excel at translating news media from Mandarin to English but struggle with literary texts, with DeepSeek particularly preserving cultural nuances better than GPT-4o. Persistent challenges like maintaining classical references and figurative expressions remain unsolved across all tested models.
content:
arXiv:2603.09998v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Although Large Language Models (LLMs) have exceptional performance in machine translation, only a limited systematic assessment of translation quality has been done. The challenge lies in automated frameworks, as human-expert-based evaluations can be time-consuming, given the fast-evolving LLMs and the need for a diverse set of texts to ensure fair assessments of translation quality. In this paper, we utilise an automated machine learning framework featuring semantic and sentiment analysis to assess Mandarin Chinese to English translation using Google Translate and LLMs, including GPT-4, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek. We compare original and translated texts in various classes of high-profile Chinese texts, which include novel texts that span modern and classical literature, as well as news articles. As the main evaluation measures, we utilise novel similarity metrics to compare the quality of translations produced by LLMs and further evaluate them by an expert human translator. Our results indicate that the LLMs perform well in news media translation, but show divergence in their performance when applied to literary texts. Although GPT-4o and DeepSeek demonstrated better semantic conservation in complex situations, DeepSeek demonstrated better performance in preserving cultural subtleties and grammatical rendering. Nevertheless, the subtle challenges in translation remain: maintaining cultural details, classical references and figurative expressions remain an open problem for all the models.

zh.title: 大型语言模型在汉英机器翻译中的自动化评估
zh.summary: 系统评估表明，大型语言模型在翻译普通话新闻媒体方面表现出色，但在翻译文学文本时存在困难，其中DeepSeek在保留文化微妙之处方面表现优于GPT-4o。跨所有测试模型，保持古典典故和修辞表达等持续挑战仍未得到解决。

fr.title: Évaluation automatisée des LLM pour la traduction automatique efficace du chinois mandarin vers l'anglais
fr.summary: Une évaluation systématique révèle que les LLM excellent dans la traduction des médias d'actualités du mandarin vers l'anglais mais éprouvent des difficultés avec les textes littéraires, DeepSeek préservant particulièrement mieux les nuances culturelles que GPT-4o. Les défis persistants tels que le maintien des références classiques et des expressions figuratives restent non résolus dans tous les modèles testés.

de.title: Automatisierte Bewertung von LLMs für effektive maschinelle Übersetzung vom Mandarin-Chinesisch ins Englische
de.summary: Eine systematische Bewertung zeigt, dass LLMs bei der Übersetzung von Nachrichtenmedien vom Mandarin ins Englische hervorragend sind, aber bei literarischen Texten Schwierigkeiten haben, wobei DeepSeek kulturelle Nuancen besonders besser bewahrt als GPT-4o. Anhaltende Herausforderungen wie die Aufrechterhaltung klassischer Bezüge und bildlicher Ausdrücke bleiben bei allen getesteten Modellen ungelöst.

es.title: Evaluación automatizada de LLMs para una traducción automática efectiva del chino mandarín al inglés
es.summary: Una evaluación sistemática revela que los LLM sobresalen en la traducción de medios informativos del mandarín al inglés pero luchan con textos literarios, siendo DeepSeek particularmente mejor preservando matices culturales que GPT-4o. Los desafíos persistentes como mantener referencias clásicas y expresiones figuradas siguen sin resolver en todos los modelos probados.

title: A Retrieval-Augmented Language Assistant for Unmanned Aircraft Safety Assessment and Regulatory Compliance
summary: A retrieval-augmented language assistant designed for unmanned aircraft safety ensures regulatory compliance by grounding responses in authoritative sources and preventing common LLM failure modes like hallucination. The system prioritizes human oversight and traceability in safety-critical aviation contexts.
content:
arXiv:2603.09999v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper presents the design and validation of a retrieval-based assistant that supports safety assessment, certification activities, and regulatory compliance for unmanned aircraft systems. The work is motivated by the growing complexity of drone operations and the increasing effort required by applicants and aviation authorities to apply established assessment frameworks, including the Specific Operations Risk Assessment and the Pre-defined Risk Assessment, in a consistent and efficient manner. The proposed approach uses a controlled text-based architecture that relies exclusively on authoritative regulatory sources. To enable traceable and auditable outputs, the assistant grounds each response in retrieved passages and enforces citation-driven generation. System-level controls address common failure modes of generative models, including fabricated statements, unsupported inferences, and unclear provenance, by separating evidence storage from language generation and by adopting conservative behavior when supporting documentation is insufficient. The assistant is intentionally limited to decision support; it does not replace expert judgment and it does not make autonomous determinations. Instead, it accelerates context-specific information retrieval and synthesis to improve document preparation and review while preserving human responsibility for critical conclusions. The architecture is implemented using established open-source components, and key choices in retrieval strategy, interaction constraints, and response policies are evaluated for suitability in safety-sensitive regulatory environments. The paper provides technical and operational guidance for integrating retrieval-based assistants into aviation oversight workflows while maintaining accountability, traceability, and regulatory compliance.

zh.title: 用于无人飞行器安全评估和监管合规的检索增强型语言助手
zh.summary: 为无人飞行器安全设计的检索增强型语言助手通过在权威来源中进行回应并防止幻觉等常见LLM故障模式来确保监管合规。该系统在安全关键的航空环境中优先考虑人类监督和可追溯性。

fr.title: Un Assistant de Langage Augmenté par la Récupération pour l'Évaluation de la Sécurité des Aéronefs sans Pilote et la Conformité Réglementaire
fr.summary: Un assistant de langage augmenté par la récupération, conçu pour la sécurité des aéronefs sans pilote, assure la conformité réglementaire en ancrant les réponses dans des sources faisant autorité et en prévenant les modes de défaillance courants des LLM comme les hallucinations. Le système priorise la surveillance humaine et la traçabilité dans les contextes aéronautiques critiques pour la sécurité.

de.title: Ein Retrieval-Augmented Language Assistant für Sicherheitsbewertung und behördliche Einhaltung von unbemannten Flugzeugen
de.summary: Ein Retrieval-Augmented Language Assistant, der für die Sicherheit von unbemannten Flugzeugen entwickelt wurde, gewährleistet die behördliche Einhaltung durch Verankerung von Antworten in maßgeblichen Quellen und Verhinderung häufiger LLM-Ausfallmoden wie Halluzinationen. Das System priorisiert menschliche Aufsicht und Rückverfolgbarkeit in sicherheitskritischen Luftfahrtkontexten.

es.title: Un Asistente de Lenguaje Aumentado por Recuperación para la Evaluación de Seguridad de Aeronaves no Tripuladas y Cumplimiento Regulatorio
es.summary: Un asistente de lenguaje aumentado por recuperación diseñado para la seguridad de aeronaves no tripuladas asegura el cumplimiento regulatorio anclando respuestas en fuentes autorizadas y previniendo modos de fallo comunes de LLM como alucinaciones. El sistema prioriza la supervisión humana y la trazabilidad en contextos aeronáuticos críticos para la seguridad.

title: Leveraging Wikidata for Geographically Informed Sociocultural Bias Dataset Creation: Application to Latin America
summary: Researchers created LatamQA, a 26k-question dataset in Spanish and Portuguese, to assess LLM cultural biases toward Latin America and found models perform better in their original language while exhibiting significant knowledge gaps about Latam cultures. The dataset addresses a critical lack of resources for detecting biases in non-English contexts.
content:
arXiv:2603.10001v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit inequalities with respect to various cultural contexts. Most prominent open-weights models are trained on Global North data and show prejudicial behavior towards other cultures. Moreover, there is a notable lack of resources to detect biases in non-English languages, especially from Latin America (Latam), a continent containing various cultures, even though they share a common cultural ground. We propose to leverage the content of Wikipedia, the structure of the Wikidata knowledge graph, and expert knowledge from social science in order to create a dataset of question/answer (Q/As) pairs, based on the different popular and social cultures of various Latin American countries. We create the LatamQA database of over 26k questions and associated answers extracted from 26k Wikipedia articles, and transformed into multiple-choice questions (MCQ) in Spanish and Portuguese, in turn translated to English. We use this MCQ to quantify the degree of knowledge of various LLMs and find out (i) a discrepancy in performances between the Latam countries, ones being easier than others for the majority of the models, (ii) that the models perform better in their original language, and (iii) that Iberian Spanish culture is better known than Latam one.

zh.title: 利用维基数据创建地理知情的社会文化偏见数据集：拉丁美洲应用
zh.summary: 研究人员创建了LatamQA，这是一个包含26,000个问题的西班牙语和葡萄牙语数据集，以评估LLM对拉丁美洲的文化偏见，发现模型在其原始语言中表现更好，但在拉丁美洲文化方面存在重大知识差距。该数据集解决了在非英语环境中检测偏见资源严重缺乏的问题。

fr.title: Tirer parti de Wikidata pour la Création de Datasets de Biais Socioculturels Informés Géographiquement : Application à l'Amérique Latine
fr.summary: Des chercheurs ont créé LatamQA, un ensemble de données de 26 000 questions en espagnol et portugais, pour évaluer les biais culturels des LLM envers l'Amérique latine et ont constaté que les modèles fonctionnent mieux dans leur langue d'origine tout en présentant des lacunes importantes dans les connaissances sur les cultures latino-américaines. L'ensemble de données aborde une pénurie critique de ressources pour détecter les biais dans les contextes non-anglais.

de.title: Nutzung von Wikidata für die Erstellung von geografisch informierten Datensätzen zu soziokulturellen Vorurteilen: Anwendung auf Lateinamerika
de.summary: Forscher erstellten LatamQA, einen Datensatz mit 26.000 Fragen in Spanisch und Portugiesisch, um LLM-Kulturvorurteile gegenüber Lateinamerika zu bewerten und stellten fest, dass Modelle in ihrer ursprünglichen Sprache besser funktionieren, aber erhebliche Wissenslücken über lateinamerikanische Kulturen aufweisen. Der Datensatz behebt einen kritischen Mangel an Ressourcen zur Erkennung von Vorurteilen in nicht-englischsprachigen Kontexten.

es.title: Aprovechando Wikidata para la Creación de Conjuntos de Datos de Sesgos Socioculturales Informados Geográficamente: Aplicación a América Latina
es.summary: Investigadores crearon LatamQA, un conjunto de datos de 26,000 preguntas en español y portugués, para evaluar los sesgos culturales de los LLM hacia América Latina y encontraron que los modelos funcionan mejor en su idioma original mientras exhiben brechas significativas de conocimiento sobre culturas latinoamericanas. El conjunto de datos aborda la escasez crítica de recursos para detectar sesgos en contextos no ingleses.

title: SpreadsheetArena: Decomposing Preference in LLM Generation of Spreadsheet Workbooks
summary: SpreadsheetArena reveals that LLM-generated spreadsheets vary significantly in quality depending on use case, with domain-specific tasks like finance showing poor alignment to best practices even among top-ranked models. The evaluation framework addresses unique challenges of assessing complex, multi-dimensional structured outputs.
content:
arXiv:2603.10002v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly tasked with producing and manipulating structured artifacts. We consider the task of end-to-end spreadsheet generation, where language models are prompted to produce spreadsheet artifacts to satisfy users' explicit and implicit constraints, specified in natural language. We introduce SpreadsheetArena, a platform for evaluating models' performance on the task via blind pairwise evaluations of LLM-generated spreadsheet workbooks. As with other complex, open-ended tasks, relevant evaluation criteria can vary substantially across use cases and prompts, often in ways that are difficult to formalize. Compared to general chat or text generation settings, spreadsheet generation presents unique challenges and opportunities: the task output structure is well-defined and multi-dimensional, and there are often complex considerations around interactivity and layout. Among other findings, we observe that stylistic, structural, and functional features of preferred spreadsheets vary substantially across use cases, and expert evaluations of spreadsheets for finance prompts suggests that even highly ranked arena models do not reliably produce spreadsheets aligned with domain-specific best practices. Our hope is that our work prompts further study of end-to-end spreadsheet generation as a challenging and interesting category of complex, open-ended tasks for LLMs. Our live arena is hosted at https://spreadsheetarena.ai.

zh.title: SpreadsheetArena：分解大型语言模型生成电子表格工作簿中的偏好
zh.summary: SpreadsheetArena揭示了LLM生成的电子表格质量根据用例差异很大，特别是金融等领域特定任务即使在排名最高的模型中也显示出与最佳实践的糟糕对齐。该评估框架解决了评估复杂多维结构化输出的独特挑战。

fr.title: SpreadsheetArena : Décomposer les préférences dans la génération de classeurs de feuilles de calcul par LLM
fr.summary: SpreadsheetArena révèle que les feuilles de calcul générées par LLM varient considérablement en qualité selon le cas d'usage, les tâches spécifiques au domaine comme la finance montrant un mauvais alignement avec les meilleures pratiques même parmi les modèles les mieux classés. Le cadre d'évaluation aborde les défis uniques de l'évaluation des résultats structurés complexes et multidimensionnels.

de.title: SpreadsheetArena: Zerlegung von Präferenzen bei der LLM-Generierung von Tabellenkalkulationsarbeitsmappen
de.summary: SpreadsheetArena zeigt, dass von LLMs generierte Tabellenkalkulationen je nach Anwendungsfall erheblich in der Qualität variieren, wobei domänenspezifische Aufgaben wie Finanzen selbst bei Top-Modellen eine schlechte Übereinstimmung mit Best Practices aufweisen. Das Evaluierungsrahmenwerk befasst sich mit den einzigartigen Herausforderungen bei der Bewertung komplexer, mehrdimensionaler strukturierter Ausgaben.

es.title: SpreadsheetArena: Descomposición de preferencias en la generación de libros de trabajo de hojas de cálculo por LLM
es.summary: SpreadsheetArena revela que las hojas de cálculo generadas por LLM varían significativamente en calidad según el caso de uso, con tareas específicas del dominio como finanzas mostrando una pobre alineación con las mejores prácticas incluso entre los modelos mejor clasificados. El marco de evaluación aborda desafíos únicos en la evaluación de resultados estructurados complejos y multidimensionales.

title: SENS-ASR: Semantic Embedding injection in Neural-transducer for Streaming Automatic Speech Recognition
summary: SENS-ASR improves streaming automatic speech recognition by supplementing acoustic information with semantic context extracted from past frames, trained through knowledge distillation. Testing on standard datasets shows significant word error rate improvements in low-latency streaming scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.10005v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Many Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) applications require streaming processing of the audio data. In streaming mode, ASR systems need to start transcribing the input stream before it is complete, i.e., the systems have to process a stream of inputs with a limited (or no) future context. Compared to offline mode, this reduction of the future context degrades the performance of Streaming-ASR systems, especially while working with low-latency constraint. In this work, we present SENS-ASR, an approach to enhance the transcription quality of Streaming-ASR by reinforcing the acoustic information with semantic information. This semantic information is extracted from the available past frame-embeddings by a context module. This module is trained using knowledge distillation from a sentence embedding Language Model fine-tuned on the training dataset transcriptions. Experiments on standard datasets show that SENS-ASR significantly improves the Word Error Rate on small-chunk streaming scenarios.

zh.title: SENS-ASR：神经转导器中的语义嵌入注入用于流式自动语音识别
zh.summary: SENS-ASR通过补充声学信息与从过去帧提取的语义上下文（通过知识蒸馏训练）来改进流式自动语音识别。在标准数据集上的测试显示在低延迟流式场景中词错误率显著改进。

fr.title: SENS-ASR : Injection d'enchâssement sémantique dans le transducteur neuronal pour la reconnaissance automatique de la parole en continu
fr.summary: SENS-ASR améliore la reconnaissance automatique de la parole en continu en supplémentant les informations acoustiques par un contexte sémantique extrait des trames passées, entraîné par distillation de connaissances. Les tests sur des ensembles de données standard montrent des améliorations significatives du taux d'erreur de mot dans les scénarios de flux à faible latence.

de.title: SENS-ASR: Semantische Einbettungsinjection in Neural-Transducer für Streaming-Spracherkennung
de.summary: SENS-ASR verbessert die Streaming-Spracherkennung durch Ergänzung akustischer Informationen mit semantischem Kontext, der aus früheren Frames extrahiert und durch Wissensdestillation trainiert wird. Tests an Standard-Datensätzen zeigen erhebliche Verbesserungen der Wortfehlerrate in Low-Latency-Streaming-Szenarien.

es.title: SENS-ASR: Inyección de incrustación semántica en transductor neuronal para reconocimiento de voz automático de transmisión
es.summary: SENS-ASR mejora el reconocimiento de voz automático de transmisión suplementando información acústica con contexto semántico extraído de fotogramas anteriores, entrenado mediante destilación de conocimiento. Las pruebas en conjuntos de datos estándar muestran mejoras significativas en la tasa de error de palabras en escenarios de transmisión de baja latencia.

title: GATech at AbjadMed: Bidirectional Encoders vs. Causal Decoders: Insights from 82-Class Arabic Medical Classification
summary: Fine-tuned bidirectional encoders significantly outperform causal decoders for Arabic medical text classification, demonstrating that global context is superior to next-token prediction for semantic precision in specialized domains. Class imbalance and label noise present ongoing challenges despite strong performance metrics.
content:
arXiv:2603.10008v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper presents system description for Arabic medical text classification across 82 distinct categories. Our primary architecture utilizes a fine-tuned AraBERTv2 encoder enhanced with a hybrid pooling strategies, combining attention and mean representations, and multi-sample dropout for robust regularization. We systematically benchmark this approach against a suite of multilingual and Arabic-specific encoders, as well as several large-scale causal decoders, including zero-shot re-ranking via Llama 3.3 70B and feature extraction from Qwen 3B hidden states. Our findings demonstrate that specialized bidirectional encoders significantly outperform causal decoders in capturing the precise semantic boundaries required for fine-grained medical text classification. We show that causal decoders, optimized for next-token prediction, produce sequence-biased embeddings that are less effective for categorization compared to the global context captured by bidirectional attention. Despite significant class imbalance and label noise identified within the training data, our results highlight the superior semantic compression of fine-tuned encoders for specialized Arabic NLP tasks. Final performance metrics on the test set, including Accuracy and Macro-F1, are reported and discussed.

zh.title: GATech在AbjadMed：双向编码器与因果解码器：来自82类阿拉伯医学分类的见解
zh.summary: 微调的双向编码器在阿拉伯医学文本分类中显著优于因果解码器，证明在专业领域中全局上下文优于下一个标记预测以实现语义精度。尽管性能指标强劲，类不平衡和标签噪声仍然是持续的挑战。

fr.title: GATech à AbjadMed : Encodeurs bidirectionnels vs décodeurs causaux : Perspectives de la classification médicale arabe à 82 classes
fr.summary: Les encodeurs bidirectionnels affinés surpassent considérablement les décodeurs causaux pour la classification de textes médicaux arabes, démontrant que le contexte global est supérieur à la prédiction du token suivant pour la précision sémantique dans les domaines spécialisés. Le déséquilibre des classes et le bruit des étiquettes restent des défis persistants malgré des métriques de performance fortes.

de.title: GATech bei AbjadMed: Bidirektionale Encoder vs. kausale Decoder: Erkenntnisse aus der 82-Klassen-arabischen medizinischen Klassifizierung
de.summary: Feinabgestimmte bidirektionale Encoder übertreffen Kausal-Decoder erheblich bei der arabischen medizinischen Textklassifizierung und zeigen, dass globaler Kontext der Vorhersage des nächsten Tokens für semantische Genauigkeit in spezialisierten Bereichen überlegen ist. Klassenunausgewogenheit und Label-Rauschen bleiben trotz starker Leistungsmetriken eine anhaltende Herausforderung.

es.title: GATech en AbjadMed: Codificadores bidireccionales frente a decodificadores causales: Perspectivas de la clasificación médica árabe de 82 clases
es.summary: Los codificadores bidireccionales ajustados superan significativamente a los decodificadores causales en la clasificación de textos médicos árabes, demostrando que el contexto global es superior a la predicción del siguiente token para la precisión semántica en dominios especializados. El desequilibrio de clases y el ruido de etiquetas siguen siendo desafíos persistentes a pesar de métricas de desempeño sólidas.

title: Personalized Group Relative Policy Optimization for Heterogenous Preference Alignment
summary: Personalized GRPO addresses limitations in standard RLHF by decoupling advantage estimation from batch statistics, allowing models to learn distinct user preferences rather than defaulting to dominant ones. The framework achieves faster convergence and higher rewards while maintaining diverse preference alignment.
content:
arXiv:2603.10009v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite their sophisticated general-purpose capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to align with diverse individual preferences because standard post-training methods, like Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), optimize for a single, global objective. While Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is a widely adopted on-policy reinforcement learning framework, its group-based normalization implicitly assumes that all samples are exchangeable, inheriting this limitation in personalized settings. This assumption conflates distinct user reward distributions and systematically biases learning toward dominant preferences while suppressing minority signals. To address this, we introduce Personalized GRPO (P-GRPO), a novel alignment framework that decouples advantage estimation from immediate batch statistics. By normalizing advantages against preference-group-specific reward histories rather than the concurrent generation group, P-GRPO preserves the contrastive signal necessary for learning distinct preferences. We evaluate P-GRPO across diverse tasks and find that it consistently achieves faster convergence and higher rewards than standard GRPO, thereby enhancing its ability to recover and align with heterogeneous preference signals. Our results demonstrate that accounting for reward heterogeneity at the optimization level is essential for building models that faithfully align with diverse human preferences without sacrificing general capabilities.

zh.title: 个性化群体相对策略优化用于异构偏好对齐
zh.summary: 个性化GRPO通过将优势估计与批量统计解耦来解决标准RLHF的局限性，允许模型学习不同的用户偏好而非默认为主导偏好。该框架实现了更快的收敛和更高的奖励，同时保持多样化的偏好对齐。

fr.title: Optimisation de Politique Relative de Groupe Personnalisée pour l'Alignement des Préférences Hétérogènes
fr.summary: GRPO personnalisé résout les limitations du RLHF standard en découplant l'estimation des avantages des statistiques par lot, permettant aux modèles d'apprendre des préférences utilisateur distinctes plutôt que de se fier aux dominantes. Le cadre atteint une convergence plus rapide et des récompenses plus élevées tout en maintenant un alignement diversifié des préférences.

de.title: Personalisierte Group-Relative-Policy-Optimierung für heterogene Präferenzausrichtung
de.summary: Personalisiertes GRPO löst Einschränkungen des Standard-RLHF durch Entkopplung der Vorteilsschätzung von Batch-Statistiken, sodass Modelle unterschiedliche Nutzerpräferenzen erlernen können, anstatt sich auf dominante zu verlassen. Das Framework erreicht schnellere Konvergenz und höhere Belohnungen, während gleichzeitig diverse Präferenzausrichtung erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Optimización de Política Relativa de Grupo Personalizada para Alineación de Preferencias Heterogéneas
es.summary: GRPO personalizado aborda las limitaciones del RLHF estándar al desacoplar la estimación de ventajas de las estadísticas por lotes, permitiendo que los modelos aprendan preferencias de usuario distintas en lugar de adoptar las dominantes. El marco logra una convergencia más rápida y recompensas más altas mientras mantiene una alineación de preferencias diversa.

title: FERRET: Framework for Expansion Reliant Red Teaming
summary: FERRET introduces a multi-phase automated red teaming framework that generates adversarial conversations through horizontal, vertical, and meta expansions to systematically identify vulnerabilities in multimodal AI models. The approach demonstrates superior performance compared to existing automated red teaming methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.10010v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce a multi-faceted automated red teaming framework in which the goal is to generate multi-modal adversarial conversations that would break a target model and introduce various expansions that would result in more effective and efficient adversarial conversations. The introduced expansions include: 1. Horizontal expansion in which the goal is for the red team model to self-improve and generate more effective conversation starters that would shape a conversation. 2. Vertical expansion in which the goal is to take these conversation starters that are discovered in the horizontal expansion phase and expand them into effective multi-modal conversations and 3. Meta expansion in which the goal is for the red team model to discover more effective multi-modal attack strategies during the course of a conversation. We call our framework FERRET (Framework for Expansion Reliant Red Teaming) and compare it with various existing automated red teaming approaches. In our experiments, we demonstrate the effectiveness of FERRET in generating effective multi-modal adversarial conversations and its superior performance against existing state of the art approaches.

zh.title: FERRET：扩展依赖红队测试框架
zh.summary: FERRET引入了一个多阶段自动化红队框架，通过水平、垂直和元扩展生成对抗性对话，以系统地识别多模态AI模型中的漏洞。该方法相比现有自动化红队方法表现出优异性能。

fr.title: FERRET : Cadre de Tests de Sécurité par Expansion Dépendante
fr.summary: FERRET introduit un cadre de tests de sécurité automatisé multi-phases qui génère des conversations adversariales à travers des extensions horizontales, verticales et méta pour identifier systématiquement les vulnérabilités dans les modèles IA multimodaux. L'approche démontre une performance supérieure par rapport aux méthodes de tests de sécurité automatisées existantes.

de.title: FERRET: Framework für automatisierte Sicherheitstests mit Expansionsabhängigkeit
de.summary: FERRET führt ein mehrphasiges automatisiertes Sicherheitsprüf-Framework ein, das gegnerische Gespräche durch horizontale, vertikale und Meta-Erweiterungen generiert, um systematisch Sicherheitslücken in multimodalen KI-Modellen zu identifizieren. Der Ansatz zeigt überlegene Leistung im Vergleich zu bestehenden automatisierten Sicherheitsprüfmethoden.

es.title: FERRET: Marco para Pruebas de Seguridad con Expansión Dependiente
es.summary: FERRET introduce un marco de pruebas de seguridad automatizado multifase que genera conversaciones adversariales a través de expansiones horizontales, verticales y meta para identificar sistemáticamente vulnerabilidades en modelos de IA multimodal. El enfoque demuestra un desempeño superior en comparación con métodos de pruebas de seguridad automatizadas existentes.

title: Measuring and Eliminating Refusals in Military Large Language Models
summary: Researchers found that mainstream LLMs refuse legitimate military-domain queries at rates as high as 98%, prompting development of specialized military models; however, techniques to reduce refusals showed trade-offs with performance on other military tasks. The study highlights tension between safety constraints and operational needs in defense applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.10012v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Military Large Language Models (LLMs) must provide accurate information to the warfighter in time-critical and dangerous situations. However, today's LLMs are imbued with safety behaviors that cause the LLM to refuse many legitimate queries in the military domain, particularly those related to violence, terrorism, or military technology. Our gold benchmark for assessing refusal rates, which was developed by veterans of the US Army and special forces, is to our knowledge the first dataset of its kind. We present results for refusal and deflection rates on 31 public models and 3 military models. We observe hard rejection rates as high as 98.2% and soft deflection rates ranging from 0% to 21.3%. We also present results on two additional synthetic datasets and show their correlations with the gold dataset. Finally, we perform abliteration using the Heretic library on a military-tuned gpt-oss-20b model, showing an absolute increase in answer rate of 66.5 points but an average relative decrease of 2% on other military tasks. In our concluding remarks, we argue for deeper specialization, including with mid-training and end-to-end post-training, to achieve zero refusals and maximum military task accuracy for closed military models.

zh.title: 衡量和消除军事大型语言模型中的拒绝
zh.summary: 研究人员发现主流LLM拒绝合法军事领域查询的比率高达98%，促使专门军事模型的开发；然而，减少拒绝的技术在其他军事任务上表现出权衡。该研究突出了防御应用中安全约束和运营需求之间的紧张关系。

fr.title: Mesurer et Éliminer les Refus dans les Grands Modèles de Langage Militaires
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont découvert que les LLM grand public refusent les requêtes légitimes du domaine militaire à des taux aussi élevés que 98%, incitant le développement de modèles militaires spécialisés ; cependant, les techniques de réduction des refus ont montré des compromis sur les performances pour d'autres tâches militaires. L'étude met en lumière la tension entre les contraintes de sécurité et les besoins opérationnels dans les applications de défense.

de.title: Messung und Beseitigung von Verweigerungen in militärischen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Forscher stellten fest, dass Mainstream-LLMs legitime Anfragen im Militärbereich mit Raten von bis zu 98% ablehnen, was zur Entwicklung spezialisierter Militärmodelle führt; jedoch zeigten Techniken zur Verringerung von Ablehnungen Kompromisse bei der Leistung in anderen Militäraufgaben. Die Studie hebt die Spannung zwischen Sicherheitseinschränkungen und operativen Anforderungen in Verteidigungsanwendungen hervor.

es.title: Medición y Eliminación de Rechazos en Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje Militares
es.summary: Los investigadores descubrieron que los LLM convencionales rechazan consultas legítimas del dominio militar a tasas tan altas como el 98%, impulsando el desarrollo de modelos militares especializados; sin embargo, las técnicas para reducir los rechazos mostraron compensaciones en el desempeño en otras tareas militares. El estudio destaca la tensión entre restricciones de seguridad y necesidades operacionales en aplicaciones de defensa.
